75	# OFFLINE deterministic compiler stub — derives a contract from S0 candidates
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	
78	class _OfflineStubClient:
79	    """Deterministic, no-network compiler. Returns a contract built ONLY from the
80	    span-verified S0 candidates for the prompt (never inventing a hard term), then
81	    a minimal plan. Mimics a well-behaved compiler so the smoke exercises the real
82	    validation + autonomous-disclosure + hashing path.
83	    """
84	
85	    def __init__(self, prompt: str) -> None:
86	        self._prompt = prompt
87	        self._candidates = reconcile_candidates(prompt)
88	
89	    async def generate(self, prompt, system="", max_tokens=4096, temperature=0.0, **_):
90	        class _R:
91	            def __init__(self, c):
92	                self.content = c
93	
94	        if system.startswith("You are the POLARIS Research Contract"):
95	            return _R(json.dumps(self._build_contract()))
96	        return _R(json.dumps(self._build_plan()))
97	
98	    def _build_contract(self) -> dict:
99	        p = self._prompt
100	        objective = [{
101	            "term_id": "objective.question", "dimension": "objective.question",
102	            "value": p.strip(), "origin": "explicit", "force": "open",
103	            "spans": [{"start": 0, "end": len(p), "quote": p}],
104	        }]
105	        scope, coverage, assumptions = [], [], []
106	        n = 0
107	        for c in self._candidates:
108	            n += 1
109	            tid = f"scope.cand_{n}"
110	            # a candidate is span-verified iff it carries a verbatim prompt span
111	            span_ok = bool(c.spans) and all(
112	                p[s.start:s.end] == s.quote for s in c.spans
113	            )
114	            # HARD only when the deterministic source marked hard AND we have a
115	            # real span; else preference. (No invention.)
116	            force = "hard" if (c.force == "hard" and span_ok) else "preference"
117	            origin = "explicit" if span_ok else "inferred"
118	            spans = [{"start": s.start, "end": s.end, "quote": s.quote} for s in c.spans]
119	            term = {
120	                "term_id": tid, "dimension": c.dimension, "value": c.value,
121	                "origin": origin, "force": force, "spans": spans,
122	                "rationale": f"S0 candidate ({c.origin})",
123	            }
124	            if c.dimension.startswith("content"):
125	                coverage.append({
126	                    "requirement_id": tid, "kind": "topic",
127	                    "statement": term, "required": False,
128	                })
129	            else:
130	                scope.append(term)
131	            if origin in ("inferred", "policy_default"):
132	                assumptions.append({
133	                    "assumption_id": f"asm_{n}",
134	                    "statement": f"kept {c.dimension}={c.value!r} as open/preference "
135	                                 f"(no verbatim span to make it explicit)",
136	                    "affected_term_ids": [tid], "origin": "inferred",
137	                })
138	        return {"contract": {
139	            "objective": objective, "scope": scope, "coverage": coverage,
140	            "assumptions": assumptions, "complexity": "smoke",
141	        }}
142	
143	    def _build_plan(self) -> dict:
144	        return {"plan": {"threads": [], "query_intents": [], "coverage_matrix": [],
145	                         "budget": {}, "stop_conditions": []}}
146	
147	
148	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
149	# Audit one prompt
150	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
151	
152	def _audit_one(prompt_id: str, prompt: str, *, live: bool) -> dict:
153	    client = None if live else _OfflineStubClient(prompt)
154	    result = asyncio.run(run_research_planning_gate(
155	        prompt, mode="autonomous", client=client,
156	    ))
157	    contract = result.contract
158	
159	    # PROPERTY 1: no invented hard constraint (deterministic validator clean of
160	    # the no-invention/span codes).
161	    errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt)
162	    invention_codes = {"hard_not_explicit", "explicit_without_span", "span_quote_mismatch"}
163	    invented = [e.to_dict() for e in errors if e.code in invention_codes]
164	
165	    hard_terms = contract.hard_terms()
166	    bad_hard = [
167	        t.term_id for t in hard_terms
168	        if t.origin not in HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS
169	        or (t.origin == "explicit" and not any(s.matches_prompt(prompt) for s in t.spans))
170	    ]
171	
172	    # PROPERTY 2: every inferred term with a value has an assumption record.
173	    disclosed = set()
174	    for a in contract.assumptions:
175	        disclosed.update(a.affected_term_ids)
176	    undisclosed = [
177	        t.term_id for t in contract.all_terms()
178	        if t.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS
179	        and t.value not in (None, "", [], {})
180	        and t.term_id
181	        and t.term_id not in disclosed
182	    ]
183	
184	    # autonomous invariant
185	    autonomous_ok = (result.needs_input is False and result.state in ("auto_pinned", "unsatisfiable"))
186	
187	    passed = not invented and not bad_hard and not undisclosed and autonomous_ok
188	    return {
189	        "id": prompt_id,
190	        "prompt_head": prompt[:60].replace("\n", " "),
191	        "state": result.state,
192	        "needs_input": result.needs_input,
193	        "n_terms": len(contract.all_terms()),
194	        "n_hard": len(hard_terms),
195	        "n_assumptions": len(contract.assumptions),
196	        "n_coverage": len(contract.coverage),
197	        "compiler_degraded": contract.compiler_degraded,
198	        "invented_hard": invented + [{"bad_hard_term": t} for t in bad_hard],
199	        "undisclosed_inferred": undisclosed,
200	        "autonomous_ok": autonomous_ok,
201	        "PASS": passed,
202	    }
203	
204	
205	def main() -> int:
206	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
207	    ap.add_argument("--all", action="store_true", help="all 100 prompts (else the 8 smoke ids)")
208	    ap.add_argument("--live", action="store_true", help="use the real policy model (monitored job only)")
209	    args = ap.parse_args()
210	
211	    rows = [json.loads(l) for l in _QUERY_JSONL.read_text().splitlines() if l.strip()]
212	    if not args.all:
213	        rows = [r for r in rows if str(r["id"]) in _SMOKE_IDS]
214	
215	    results = [_audit_one(str(r["id"]), r["prompt"], live=args.live) for r in rows]
216	
217	    n_pass = sum(1 for r in results if r["PASS"])
218	    print(json.dumps({
219	        "mode": "LIVE" if args.live else "OFFLINE_STUB",
220	        "n_prompts": len(results),
221	        "n_pass": n_pass,
222	        "n_fail": len(results) - n_pass,
223	        "results": results,
224	    }, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
225	    return 0 if n_pass == len(results) else 1
226	
227	
228	if __name__ == "__main__":
229	    raise SystemExit(main())
230

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""WS-0 (I-deepfix-001 #1344) — the run-launch GPU device-split config + a co-residence preflight.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS (the drb_72 GPU-OOM degrade cascade)
5	----------------------------------------------------
6	On the 2-card VM (2x RTX3090Ti, ~24GB each) the paid Gate-B run put the W5 content-relevance reranker,
7	the W6 Qwen3-Embedding-8B embedder, and the W10 consolidation NLI cross-encoder ALL on ``cuda:0`` (every
8	device env unset defaults the model to card 0). The 16GB embedder + the reranker's ~24.8GB one-pass
9	logits tensor then OOM'd, and the run SILENTLY DEGRADED: W2 semantic-relevance fell back to lexical,
10	consolidation fell to the CPU wall (under-merge), and GLM credibility-tiering fell to the rules-floor.
11	That single infra fault is the upstream root of the thin single-origin corpus (B2 Recall + Analysis).
12	
13	THE FIX (proven 2026-06-30) — a 2-card device split + a bounded W5 score chunk
14	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	  * ``PG_EMBED_DEVICE=cuda:0``            — W6 Qwen3-Embedding-8B alone on card 0 (~16GB fp16)
16	  * ``PG_RERANKER_DEVICE=cuda:1``         — W7 Qwen3-Reranker-4B on card 1
17	  * ``PG_NLI_DEVICE=cuda:1``              — FaithLens NLI cross-encoder on card 1 (off the critical path)
18	  * ``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE=cuda:1``— W10 consolidation NLI cross-encoder on card 1
19	  * ``PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK=2``— W5 reranker scores in chunks so the per-forward logits tensor
20	    stays small enough to fit alongside the co-resident embedder (faithfulness-neutral — chunked scores
21	    are byte-identical to the one-pass path; content_relevance_judge.py:442-455).
22	
23	The W5 content-relevance reranker (Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B) stays on ``cuda:0`` (its own
24	``PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_DEVICE`` default "cuda") but is BOUNDED by the score chunk, so the split leaves
25	card 0 = big embedder + bounded 0.6B reranker, card 1 = 4B reranker + two small NLI cross-encoders.
26	
27	SINGLE-CARD HOSTS (e.g. an A100-80GB)
28	-------------------------------------
29	On a host with only one visible CUDA device, a split cannot help — everything co-resides, and on an
30	80GB card it fits with headroom. The card-split warning is therefore suppressed when ``device_count < 2``.
31	The W5 score-chunk bound STILL applies (it is cheap insurance against the 24.8GB one-pass tensor even on
32	a big card), so that warning is card-count-independent.
33	
34	BUILD-ONLY / NO SPEND / NO MODEL LOAD
35	-------------------------------------
36	Pure config. ``detect_coresidence_warnings`` is a pure function over an env map + a device count (offline-
37	testable, no GPU needed). Only ``--check`` touches the GPU — a free ``torch.cuda.device_count()`` read
38	(no model load, no completion call). ``--export`` emits sourceable ``export K=V`` lines for the launcher.
39	
40	LAW VI: the recommended values ARE this file's config surface (a documented launch template), not magic
41	numbers scattered through the run path. The run path READS these envs; this helper DECLARES them.
42	
43	Usage
44	-----
45	    # print the documented launch template (human-readable)
46	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/gpu_device_split.py
47	
48	    # emit sourceable export lines for a 2-card launch
49	    source <(python scripts/dr_benchmark/gpu_device_split.py --export)
50	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py --only <slug>
51	
52	    # preflight the CURRENT env against the visible GPU topology (warns on co-residence)
53	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/gpu_device_split.py --check     # exit 0 clean, 3 == warnings emitted
54	"""
55	
56	from __future__ import annotations
57	
58	import argparse
59	import os
60	import sys
61	from typing import Mapping, Optional
62	
63	# The recommended 2-card split + the W5 score-chunk bound (the proven 2026-06-30 fix). This is the
64	# documented launch template; the run path reads each key at model-load / call time.
65	RECOMMENDED_DEVICE_SPLIT: dict[str, str] = {
66	    "PG_EMBED_DEVICE": "cuda:0",              # W6 Qwen3-Embedding-8B (embedding_service:150 / prefetch_offtopic_filter:140)
67	    "PG_RERANKER_DEVICE": "cuda:1",           # W7 Qwen3-Reranker-4B (evidence_selector:2658 / qwen_reranker_scorer:83)
68	    "PG_NLI_DEVICE": "cuda:1",                # FaithLens NLI (nli_verifier:95, off the critical path)
69	    "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE": "cuda:1",  # W10 consolidation NLI cross-encoder (consolidation_nli:73)
70	    "PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK": "2",  # W5 reranker chunked scoring (content_relevance_judge:451)
71	}
72	
73	# The four HEAVY-model device envs whose co-residence on one card is the OOM-degrade risk. (The W5
74	# content-relevance reranker uses PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_DEVICE and is bounded by the score chunk, so it
75	# is intentionally NOT in the card-balance set — the score-chunk warning covers it.)
76	_HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS: dict[str, str] = {
77	    "PG_EMBED_DEVICE": "W6 embedder (Qwen3-Embedding-8B, ~16GB fp16)",
78	    "PG_RERANKER_DEVICE": "W7 reranker (Qwen3-Reranker-4B, ~8GB)",
79	    "PG_NLI_DEVICE": "FaithLens NLI cross-encoder",
80	    "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE": "W10 consolidation NLI cross-encoder",
81	}
82	
83	# An UNSET device env lands the model on the module default card (cuda:0). That default IS the OOM trap
84	# the split fixes, so an unset env resolves to card 0 for the co-residence check (never "unknown").
85	_DEFAULT_GPU_CARD = 0
86	
87	_SCORE_CHUNK_ENV = "PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK"
88	
89	
90	def resolve_gpu_card(value: Optional[str]) -> Optional[int]:
91	    """The GPU card index a device string targets. ``cuda:N`` -> N; ``cuda`` / bare ``N`` -> that index;
92	    UNSET/empty -> the module default card 0 (the co-residence trap); ``cpu`` or an unparseable value ->
93	    None (not a GPU card, excluded from the balance check)."""
94	    v = (value or "").strip().lower()
95	    if not v:
96	        return _DEFAULT_GPU_CARD
97	    if v == "cpu":
98	        return None
99	    if v == "cuda":
100	        return 0
101	    if v.startswith("cuda:"):
102	        tail = v.split(":", 1)[1]
103	        return int(tail) if tail.isdigit() else None
104	    if v.isdigit():
105	        return int(v)
106	    return None
107	
108	
109	def detect_coresidence_warnings(
110	    env: Mapping[str, str], device_count: Optional[int]
111	) -> list[str]:
112	    """Pure preflight. Return the list of WARNING strings for the given env + visible GPU count.
113	
114	    (1) CARD CO-RESIDENCE — only when ``device_count >= 2``: if every heavy-model device env resolves to
115	        a SINGLE GPU card, the models will co-reside and risk the GPU-OOM degrade cascade; recommend the
116	        split. Suppressed on a 1-GPU host (a split cannot help there).
117	    (2) W5 SCORE-CHUNK — card-count-independent: if ``PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK`` is unset/0/negative
118	        (one-pass), the ~24.8GB one-pass logits tensor can OOM the co-resident embedder; recommend =2.
119	    """
120	    warns: list[str] = []
121	
122	    if device_count is not None and device_count >= 2:
123	        gpu_cards: set[int] = set()
124	        for name in _HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS:
125	            card = resolve_gpu_card(env.get(name))
126	            if card is not None:
127	                gpu_cards.add(card)
128	        if len(gpu_cards) <= 1:
129	            only = next(iter(gpu_cards), _DEFAULT_GPU_CARD)
130	            split = ", ".join(
131	                f"{k}={RECOMMENDED_DEVICE_SPLIT[k]}"
132	                for k in _HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS
133	                if k in RECOMMENDED_DEVICE_SPLIT
134	            )
135	            warns.append(
136	                f"[gpu-device-split WARNING] {device_count} CUDA devices visible but every heavy model "
137	                f"resolves to a SINGLE card cuda:{only} "
138	                f"({', '.join(_HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS)}) — they will co-reside and risk a GPU-OOM degrade "
139	                f"(W5 reranker + W6 embedder + W10 NLI on one card was the drb_72 root). Apply the 2-card "
140	                f"split: {split}."
141	            )
142	
143	    try:
144	        chunk = int((env.get(_SCORE_CHUNK_ENV) or "0").strip())
145	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
146	        chunk = 0
147	    if chunk <= 0:
148	        warns.append(
149	            f"[gpu-device-split WARNING] {_SCORE_CHUNK_ENV} is unset/0 (one-pass) — the W5 Qwen3-Reranker "
150	            f"scores ALL pairs in one forward pass (~24.8GB logits tensor) and can OOM the co-resident "
151	            f"embedder on cuda:0. Set {_SCORE_CHUNK_ENV}=2 (faithfulness-neutral: chunked scores are "
152	            f"byte-identical to one-pass)."
153	        )
154	    return warns
155	
156	
157	def _visible_cuda_device_count() -> Optional[int]:
158	    """A FREE ``torch.cuda.device_count()`` read (no model load, no spend). None if torch is unavailable."""
159	    try:
160	        import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
161	    except ImportError:
162	        return None
163	    try:
164	        return int(torch.cuda.device_count())
165	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a driver hiccup must never crash a preflight
166	        return None
167	
168	
169	def render_launch_template(as_export: bool = False) -> str:
170	    """The documented launch config. ``as_export=True`` -> sourceable ``export K=V`` lines; else a
171	    human-readable ``K=V  # why`` template."""
172	    lines: list[str] = []
173	    for key, value in RECOMMENDED_DEVICE_SPLIT.items():
174	        if as_export:
175	            lines.append(f"export {key}={value}")
176	        else:
177	            why = _HEAVY_MODEL_DEVICE_ENVS.get(key, "W5 reranker chunked scoring (one-pass OOM guard)")
178	            lines.append(f"{key}={value}  # {why}")
179	    return "\n".join(lines)
180	
181	
182	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
183	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="WS-0 GPU device-split launch config + co-residence preflight.")
184	    ap.add_argument("--export", action="store_true", help="emit sourceable `export K=V` lines")
185	    ap.add_argument("--check", action="store_true",
186	                    help="preflight the CURRENT env vs the visible GPU topology (exit 3 if warnings)")
187	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
188	
189	    if args.export:
190	        print(render_launch_template(as_export=True))
191	        return 0
192	
193	    if args.check:
194	        device_count = _visible_cuda_device_count()
195	        dc_note = "torch unavailable (card-split check skipped)" if device_count is None else f"{device_count} CUDA device(s)"
196	        print(f"[gpu-device-split] visible: {dc_note}")
197	        warns = detect_coresidence_warnings(os.environ, device_count)
198	        for w in warns:
199	            print(w)
200	        if not warns:
201	            print("[gpu-device-split] OK — no co-residence / one-pass OOM risk in the current env.")
202	        return 3 if warns else 0
203	
204	    # default: print the documented template.
205	    print("# WS-0 recommended 2-card GPU device split (source via --export):")
206	    print(render_launch_template(as_export=False))
207	    return 0
208	
209	
210	if __name__ == "__main__":
211	    sys.exit(main())
212

1	"""DICED harness — I-deepfix-001 Wave-2 (#1370): compose-stage ANTI-GLUE.
2	
3	Proves ``PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE`` on FROZEN box2 artifacts (read-only), seconds-level, NO fresh
4	run / NO fetch / NO GPU / NO LLM. It exercises the SMALLEST callable join unit
5	``cross_source_synthesis._join_analytical_pair`` — the exact function the composer uses to join two
6	verified single-source clauses. It builds THREE clauses as verbatim substrings of REAL box2 evidence
7	spans (so each clause is faithful-by-construction and carries a real ``[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`` token):
8	
9	  * clause_a          — ev_1226  "total publications using a Gaussian distribution"
10	  * clause_b_unrel    — ev_446   "was summed up by Informant 17"   (topically UNRELATED to clause_a)
11	  * clause_a2         — frey_osborne_computerisation  "the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations"
12	  * clause_b_rel      — ev_165   "600 detailed occupations in over 300 occupational profiles"  (RELATED: shares "detailed","occupations")
13	
14	Signal:
15	  RED  (flag OFF): the topically-UNRELATED NEUTRAL pair is GLUED with "; separately," (incoherent run-on).
16	  GREEN(flag ON) : the UNRELATED pair renders as TWO SEPARATE sentences (no "; separately,"), while the
17	                   genuinely-RELATED pair STILL joins with "; separately," (the split is targeted).
18	
19	Run: PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 PYTHONPATH=<cwd> python scripts/harness_compose_no_unrelated_glue.py
20	"""
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	
28	SCRATCHPAD = (
29	    r"C:\Users\msn\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\C--POLARIS"
30	    r"\dde5b4ec-b98b-4784-a4d2-3b7fd5d3e391\scratchpad"
31	)
32	EVIDENCE_POOL = os.path.join(SCRATCHPAD, "box2_evidence_pool.json")
33	
34	# (evidence_id, verbatim_substring) — offsets are computed against the FROZEN pool so the token is honest.
35	CLAUSE_A = ("ev_1226", "total publications using a Gaussian distribution")
36	CLAUSE_B_UNRELATED = ("ev_446", "was summed up by Informant 17")
37	CLAUSE_A2 = ("frey_osborne_computerisation", "the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations")
38	CLAUSE_B_RELATED = ("ev_165", "600 detailed occupations in over 300 occupational profiles")
39	
40	
41	def _load_pool() -> dict:
42	    with open(EVIDENCE_POOL, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
43	        rows = json.load(fh)
44	    return {r.get("evidence_id"): (r.get("direct_quote") or "") for r in rows}
45	
46	
47	def _build_clause(pool: dict, eid: str, substring: str) -> str:
48	    """Build a strict_verify-shaped clause: '<verbatim substring> [#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]', where the
49	    span offsets are the REAL character range of the verbatim substring inside box2's direct_quote."""
50	    quote = pool.get(eid, "")
51	    start = quote.find(substring)
52	    if start < 0:
53	        raise SystemExit(f"BLOCKED: substring not found verbatim in {eid}: {substring!r}")
54	    end = start + len(substring)
55	    # Capitalize the leading char so the clause reads as a sentence unit (as split_into_sentences yields).
56	    prose = substring[0].upper() + substring[1:]
57	    return f"{prose} [#ev:{eid}:{start}-{end}]"
58	
59	
60	def main() -> int:
61	    from src.polaris_graph.generator import cross_source_synthesis as css
62	
63	    pool = _load_pool()
64	    clause_a = _build_clause(pool, *CLAUSE_A)
65	    clause_b_unrel = _build_clause(pool, *CLAUSE_B_UNRELATED)
66	    clause_a2 = _build_clause(pool, *CLAUSE_A2)
67	    clause_b_rel = _build_clause(pool, *CLAUSE_B_RELATED)
68	
69	    ov_unrel = len(css._glue_content_words(clause_a) & css._glue_content_words(clause_b_unrel))
70	    ov_rel = len(css._glue_content_words(clause_a2) & css._glue_content_words(clause_b_rel))
71	
72	    print("=" * 78)
73	    print("DICED HARNESS — PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE (compose-stage anti-glue)")
74	    print("=" * 78)
75	    print(f"clause_a         : {clause_a}")
76	    print(f"clause_b_unrel   : {clause_b_unrel}")
77	    print(f"  content-word overlap(unrelated) = {ov_unrel}")
78	    print(f"clause_a2        : {clause_a2}")
79	    print(f"clause_b_rel     : {clause_b_rel}")
80	    print(f"  content-word overlap(related)   = {ov_rel}")
81	    print("-" * 78)
82	
83	    glue = "; separately,"
84	
85	    def _glued(text: str) -> bool:
86	        return glue in text
87	
88	    def _two_sentences(text: str) -> bool:
89	        # A period/terminal followed by whitespace + capital = a real sentence boundary (the production
90	        # _SENT_SPLIT_RE shape: terminal .!?] + whitespace + [A-Z0-9]).
91	        return bool(re.search(r"[.!?\]]\s+[A-Z0-9]", text))
92	
93	    # ---- RED: flag OFF (default) — the UNRELATED neutral pair is GLUED ----
94	    os.environ.pop("PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE", None)
95	    assert not css.compose_no_unrelated_glue_enabled(), "flag should default OFF"
96	    off_unrel = css._join_analytical_pair(clause_a, clause_b_unrel, "neutral")
97	    off_rel = css._join_analytical_pair(clause_a2, clause_b_rel, "neutral")
98	    print("[flag OFF]")
99	    print(f"  unrelated -> {off_unrel!r}")
100	    print(f"  related   -> {off_rel!r}")
101	
102	    red_ok = _glued(off_unrel) and _glued(off_rel)
103	    print(f"  RED expectation (both glued with '; separately,') : {'RED CONFIRMED' if red_ok else 'NOT MET'}")
104	    print("-" * 78)
105	
106	    # ---- GREEN: flag ON — the UNRELATED pair SPLITS; the RELATED pair stays glued ----
107	    os.environ["PG_COMPOSE_NO_UNRELATED_GLUE"] = "1"
108	    assert css.compose_no_unrelated_glue_enabled(), "flag should be ON"
109	    on_unrel = css._join_analytical_pair(clause_a, clause_b_unrel, "neutral")
110	    on_rel = css._join_analytical_pair(clause_a2, clause_b_rel, "neutral")
111	    print("[flag ON]")
112	    print(f"  unrelated -> {on_unrel!r}")
113	    print(f"  related   -> {on_rel!r}")
114	
115	    # Faithfulness-neutral check: same two evidence_ids survive the split.
116	    ids_off = css._distinct_ev_ids(off_unrel)
117	    ids_on = css._distinct_ev_ids(on_unrel)
118	
119	    green_unrel_split = (not _glued(on_unrel)) and _two_sentences(on_unrel)
120	    green_rel_kept = _glued(on_rel)
121	    green_ids_preserved = ids_on == ids_off and len(ids_on) == 2
122	    green_ok = green_unrel_split and green_rel_kept and green_ids_preserved
123	
124	    print(f"  unrelated split into 2 sentences, no '; separately,' : {green_unrel_split}")
125	    print(f"  related STILL glued with '; separately,'             : {green_rel_kept}")
126	    print(f"  citations preserved (same 2 ev-ids OFF vs ON)        : {green_ids_preserved} {sorted(ids_on)}")
127	    print("=" * 78)
128	
129	    if red_ok and green_ok:
130	        print("RESULT: GREEN — fix splits topically-unrelated neutral glue, keeps related pairs joined,")
131	        print("        preserves both citations; OFF is byte-identical to current behaviour.")
132	        return 0
133	    print("RESULT: FAIL — see expectations above.")
134	    return 1
135	
136	
137	if __name__ == "__main__":
138	    sys.exit(main())
139

1	"""Path-B dual-§-1.1-audit ledger schema (I-safety-002b #925 PR-3).
2	
3	The per-(system × question) JSON each auditor (Claude / Codex) writes for the dual line-by-line
4	audit. Mirrors `src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer.ClaimRow` validation so a ledger
5	that fails-closed here cannot smuggle a malformed row into the scorer. Pure stdlib + dataclasses
6	(no pydantic dep added).
7	
8	Public surface:
9	- ``Claim``       — one atomic claim audited against the FETCHED cited span.
10	- ``Coverage``    — one pre-registered gold-rubric element's coverage by THIS system's report.
11	- ``Ledger``      — the full (system, question) audit ledger written by ONE auditor.
12	- ``load_ledger`` — read+validate a ledger JSON from disk.
13	- ``dump_ledger`` — write a validated ledger JSON to disk.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import json
19	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
20	from pathlib import Path
21	from typing import Literal
22	
23	# Mirrored from claim_audit_scorer.py (PR-1) — do NOT diverge.
24	Verdict = Literal["VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE"]
25	Severity = Literal["S0", "S1", "S2", "S3"]
26	UnreachableSubtype = Literal["paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure", "source_missing"]
27	Auditor = Literal["claude", "codex", "reconciled"]
28	SystemId = Literal["polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini"]
29	
30	_VERDICTS = ("VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE")
31	_SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2", "S3")
32	_UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES = ("paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure", "source_missing")
33	# "reconciled" is the output of dual-auditor reconciliation (PR-3 reconcile.py), NOT a
34	# real auditor — the upstream auditors are recorded in audit_method.
35	_AUDITORS = ("claude", "codex", "reconciled")
36	_SYSTEMS = ("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini")
37	
38	
39	@dataclass
40	class Claim:
41	    """One atomic claim's audit row. Validation MIRRORS ClaimRow.__post_init__:
42	    UNREACHABLE requires unreachable_subtype; FABRICATED/PARTIAL require span_quote;
43	    UNSUPPORTED+cited requires span_quote OR audit_note (traceability)."""
44	
45	    claim_id: str
46	    severity: str            # "S0"|"S1"|"S2"|"S3"
47	    verdict: str             # one of _VERDICTS
48	    citation_id: str | None = None
49	    span_quote: str | None = None
50	    unreachable_subtype: str | None = None
51	    audit_note: str | None = None
52	
53	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
54	        if not self.claim_id:
55	            raise ValueError("Claim.claim_id is required")
56	        if self.severity not in _SEVERITIES:
57	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: severity {self.severity!r} not in {_SEVERITIES}")
58	        if self.verdict not in _VERDICTS:
59	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: verdict {self.verdict!r} not in {_VERDICTS}")
60	        if self.verdict == "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is None:
61	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: UNREACHABLE requires unreachable_subtype")
62	        if self.verdict != "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is not None:
63	            raise ValueError(
64	                f"{self.claim_id}: unreachable_subtype only valid for UNREACHABLE"
65	            )
66	        if (
67	            self.unreachable_subtype is not None
68	            and self.unreachable_subtype not in _UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES
69	        ):
70	            raise ValueError(
71	                f"{self.claim_id}: unreachable_subtype {self.unreachable_subtype!r} "
72	                f"not in {_UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES}"
73	            )
74	        if self.verdict in ("FABRICATED", "PARTIAL") and not self.span_quote:
75	            raise ValueError(
76	                f"{self.claim_id}: {self.verdict} requires a span_quote (the refuting/partial span)"
77	            )
78	        if (
79	            self.verdict == "UNSUPPORTED"
80	            and self.citation_id
81	            and not (self.span_quote or self.audit_note)
82	        ):
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                f"{self.claim_id}: UNSUPPORTED+cited requires a span_quote or audit_note (traceability)"
85	            )
86	
87	
88	@dataclass
89	class Coverage:
90	    """One pre-registered gold-rubric element's coverage row, produced in the SAME
91	    pass as the per-claim audit (Codex PR-3 design answer B)."""
92	
93	    element_id: str
94	    covered: bool
95	    citation_supported: bool
96	    auditor_note: str | None = None
97	
98	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
99	        if not self.element_id:
100	            raise ValueError("Coverage.element_id is required")
101	        # Codex PR-3 diff P2 #1: enforce real bools. JSON deserialization can yield strings
102	        # ("false", "0", "") that are truthy in Python and would silently pass scoring.
103	        for fname in ("covered", "citation_supported"):
104	            v = getattr(self, fname)
105	            if not isinstance(v, bool):
106	                raise ValueError(
107	                    f"Coverage.{fname} must be a bool, got {type(v).__name__}: {v!r}"
108	                )
109	
110	
111	@dataclass
112	class Ledger:
113	    """The full (system, question) audit ledger written by ONE auditor (claude OR codex)."""
114	
115	    system: str           # one of _SYSTEMS
116	    question_id: str      # "Q72"|"Q75"|"Q76"|"Q78"|"Q90"
117	    auditor: str          # one of _AUDITORS
118	    audit_method: str     # short description, e.g. "dual-§-1.1-line-by-line-2026-05-28"
119	    audit_timestamp_utc: str
120	    rubric_sha256: str    # the freeze_pin.txt SHA of the rubric being audited against
121	    claims: list[Claim] = field(default_factory=list)
122	    coverage: list[Coverage] = field(default_factory=list)
123	
124	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
125	        if self.system not in _SYSTEMS:
126	            raise ValueError(f"system {self.system!r} not in {_SYSTEMS}")
127	        if self.auditor not in _AUDITORS:
128	            raise ValueError(f"auditor {self.auditor!r} not in {_AUDITORS}")
129	        if not self.question_id:
130	            raise ValueError("Ledger.question_id is required")
131	        if not self.audit_method:
132	            raise ValueError("Ledger.audit_method is required (audit-protocol identity)")
133	        if not self.audit_timestamp_utc:
134	            raise ValueError("Ledger.audit_timestamp_utc is required (audit-time identity)")
135	        if not self.rubric_sha256:
136	            raise ValueError("Ledger.rubric_sha256 is required (pre-registration anchor)")
137	        # Duplicate detection: claim_id and element_id are unique within a ledger.
138	        seen_claims = set()
139	        for c in self.claims:
140	            if c.claim_id in seen_claims:
141	                raise ValueError(f"duplicate claim_id {c.claim_id!r}")
142	            seen_claims.add(c.claim_id)
143	        seen_elements = set()
144	        for cv in self.coverage:
145	            if cv.element_id in seen_elements:
146	                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element_id {cv.element_id!r}")
147	            seen_elements.add(cv.element_id)
148	
149	
150	def load_ledger(path: Path) -> Ledger:
151	    """Read a ledger JSON; validate per dataclass __post_init__; raise on any malformed row."""
152	    raw = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
153	    claims = [Claim(**c) for c in raw.get("claims", [])]
154	    coverage = [Coverage(**c) for c in raw.get("coverage", [])]
155	    head = {k: v for k, v in raw.items() if k not in ("claims", "coverage")}
156	    return Ledger(claims=claims, coverage=coverage, **head)
157	
158	
159	def dump_ledger(ledger: Ledger, path: Path) -> None:
160	    """Write a ledger JSON (validated by construction; no extra validation needed)."""
161	    out = asdict(ledger)
162	    Path(path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
163	    Path(path).write_text(
164	        json.dumps(out, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
165	        encoding="utf-8",
166	    )
167

content.text: Both mostly clean. Note: harness_compose_no_unrelated_glue.py has a hardcoded Windows path in `SCRATCHPAD`/`EVIDENCE_POOL` — that's a portability/hygiene bug but not a naming issue per the flagging categories, so out of scope. No flagged identifiers.

1	"""Pure scoring/adapter logic for the POLARIS-vs-MedHallu verification-layer benchmark.
2	
3	I-safety-002a (#924). Codex-designed run (see .codex/I-safety-002a/codex_medhallu_design.txt):
4	map MedHallu onto POLARIS's ENTAILMENT-verification layer (NOT strict_verify, which
5	needs provenance tokens and cannot run on raw MedHallu answers). Per row -> two
6	candidates (Ground Truth = faithful, Hallucinated = hallucinated); source = Question +
7	Knowledge ONLY (never the candidate answers); atomize the answer; verify each claim by
8	entailment against the Knowledge; aggregate: answer is hallucinated iff ANY substantive
9	claim is unsupported. Positive class = hallucinated.
10	
11	This module holds ONLY pure functions (no model, no I/O) so the scorer can be validated
12	on fixtures BEFORE any model run, per the Codex-APPROVE'd plan.
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
18	
19	# Codex: pin the shipped direct-verifier threshold; the headline uses the verifier's
20	# internal threshold via PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD. This mirror locks the boundary.
21	DEFAULT_NLI_FAITH_THRESHOLD = 0.65
22	
23	
24	class StrictVerifyMisuseError(RuntimeError):
25	    """Raised if anything tries to score raw MedHallu answers with strict_verify.
26	
27	    strict_verify requires POLARIS provenance tokens ([#ev:id:start-end]); raw MedHallu
28	    answers have none, so strict_verify would return no_provenance_token for every row and
29	    produce a junk 0%-specificity "everything is hallucinated" result (Codex ruling).
30	    """
31	
32	
33	@dataclass
34	class Candidate:
35	    """One scored unit: a candidate answer with its mechanical gold label."""
36	
37	    row_id: str
38	    split: str
39	    answer_text: str
40	    gold_hallucinated: bool
41	    candidate_kind: str  # "ground_truth" | "hallucinated"
42	
43	
44	def pair_row(row: dict, split: str) -> list[Candidate]:
45	    """Row -> exactly two candidates: Ground Truth (faithful) + Hallucinated (positive).
46	
47	    Fixture 1 (pairing) + the per-row clustering unit for bootstrap CIs.
48	    """
49	    row_id = str(row["row_id"])
50	    gt = (row.get("ground_truth") or "").strip()
51	    hallu = (row.get("hallucinated_answer") or "").strip()
52	    return [
53	        Candidate(row_id, split, gt, gold_hallucinated=False, candidate_kind="ground_truth"),
54	        Candidate(row_id, split, hallu, gold_hallucinated=True, candidate_kind="hallucinated"),
55	    ]
56	
57	
58	def build_source_text(question: str, knowledge: str) -> str:
59	    """Verifier source = Question + Knowledge ONLY.
60	
61	    Codex: the candidate answer (esp. Ground Truth) MUST NOT enter the evidence, or the
62	    negative label leaks. Fixture 2 (source isolation) asserts no candidate text leaks here.
63	    """
64	    return f"Question: {question.strip()}\n\nKnowledge: {knowledge.strip()}"
65	
66	
67	def assert_source_isolated(source_text: str, candidate: Candidate) -> None:
68	    """Fail-closed if a candidate answer leaked into the verifier source (Codex fixture 2)."""
69	    ans = candidate.answer_text.strip()
70	    if ans and ans in source_text:
71	        raise ValueError(
72	            f"label leak: candidate ({candidate.candidate_kind}) answer text found in "
73	            f"verifier source for row {candidate.row_id}"
74	        )
75	
76	
77	def build_evidence_object(claim: str, question: str, knowledge: str, candidate: Candidate) -> dict:
78	    """Build the single evidence dict fed to verify_evidence_nli (Codex spec).
79	
80	    source content = Question + Knowledge; statement = the claim under test. Guards source
81	    isolation. research_query is left "" by the caller to disable off-topic keyword overrides.
82	    """
83	    source_text = build_source_text(question, knowledge)
84	    assert_source_isolated(source_text, candidate)
85	    source_url = f"medhallu:{candidate.split}:{candidate.row_id}:{candidate.candidate_kind}"
86	    return {
87	        "statement": claim,
88	        "source_url": source_url,
89	        "source_title": question.strip(),
90	        "direct_quote": knowledge.strip(),
91	    }
92	
93	
94	def claim_is_faithful(prob: float, label: int, threshold: float = DEFAULT_NLI_FAITH_THRESHOLD) -> bool:
95	    """Mirror of the verifier's rule: faithful iff entailed (label==1) AND prob >= threshold.
96	
97	    Fixture 5 (threshold boundary): 0.65 passes with >=, 0.6499 fails.
98	    """
99	    return bool(label == 1) and prob >= threshold
100	
101	
102	def aggregate_answer_verdict(claim_faithful_flags: list[bool]) -> str:
103	    """Answer-level verdict from per-claim faithfulness (Codex aggregation rule).
104	
105	    - no substantive claims -> "invalid" (report separately; do not silently drop).
106	    - all claims faithful    -> "faithful".
107	    - any claim unsupported   -> "hallucinated".
108	    Fixture 4 (aggregation).
109	    """
110	    if not claim_faithful_flags:
111	        return "invalid"
112	    return "faithful" if all(claim_faithful_flags) else "hallucinated"
113	
114	
115	@dataclass
116	class Confusion:
117	    """Positive class = hallucinated."""
118	
119	    tp: int = 0  # predicted hallucinated, gold hallucinated
120	    fp: int = 0  # predicted hallucinated, gold faithful
121	    fn: int = 0  # predicted faithful, gold hallucinated
122	    tn: int = 0  # predicted faithful, gold faithful
123	    invalid: int = 0  # unparseable/empty answers (reported, not scored)
124	
125	
126	def add_prediction(conf: Confusion, predicted_verdict: str, gold_hallucinated: bool) -> None:
127	    """Fold one candidate prediction into the confusion matrix. Invalids counted aside."""
128	    if predicted_verdict == "invalid":
129	        conf.invalid += 1
130	        return
131	    pred_hallu = predicted_verdict == "hallucinated"
132	    if pred_hallu and gold_hallucinated:
133	        conf.tp += 1
134	    elif pred_hallu and not gold_hallucinated:
135	        conf.fp += 1
136	    elif (not pred_hallu) and gold_hallucinated:
137	        conf.fn += 1
138	    else:
139	        conf.tn += 1
140	
141	
142	def _safe_div(num: float, den: float) -> float:
143	    return num / den if den else 0.0
144	
145	
146	def metrics(conf: Confusion) -> dict:
147	    """F1/precision/recall/specificity/balanced-accuracy. Positive class = hallucinated.
148	
149	    Fixture 6 (confusion -> known F1).
150	    """
151	    precision = _safe_div(conf.tp, conf.tp + conf.fp)
152	    recall = _safe_div(conf.tp, conf.tp + conf.fn)  # = sensitivity
153	    specificity = _safe_div(conf.tn, conf.tn + conf.fp)
154	    f1 = _safe_div(2 * precision * recall, precision + recall)
155	    balanced_accuracy = (recall + specificity) / 2
156	    return {
157	        "f1": f1,
158	        "precision": precision,
159	        "recall": recall,
160	        "specificity": specificity,
161	        "balanced_accuracy": balanced_accuracy,
162	        "tp": conf.tp,
163	        "fp": conf.fp,
164	        "fn": conf.fn,
165	        "tn": conf.tn,
166	        "invalid": conf.invalid,
167	    }
168	
169	
170	def expected_candidate_count(n_labeled: int, n_artificial: int) -> dict:
171	    """Pairing-count math: each row -> 2 candidates (Codex fixture 7)."""
172	    return {
173	        "pqa_labeled": n_labeled * 2,
174	        "pqa_artificial": n_artificial * 2,
175	        "total": (n_labeled + n_artificial) * 2,
176	    }
177	
178	
179	@dataclass
180	class RunGuards:
181	    """Run-level guards that must hold for the headline number to be valid (Codex)."""
182	
183	    nli_model_available: bool = True
184	    llm_fallback_used: bool = False
185	    negative_control: bool = False
186	    notes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
187	
188	    def assert_headline_valid(self) -> None:
189	        """Headline run aborts if the NLI model is unavailable (Codex fixture 8: no silent
190	        fallback) or if it is a negative-control run masquerading as the headline."""
191	        if not self.nli_model_available:
192	            raise RuntimeError(
193	                "NLI model unavailable: verify_evidence_nli returned [] — abort the headline "
194	                "run, do NOT silently fall back to an LLM (Codex contamination control)."
195	            )
196	        if self.negative_control:
197	            raise RuntimeError(
198	                "negative-control run cannot be recorded as the headline artifact "
199	                "(Codex fixture 9)."
200	            )

1	"""Run POLARIS's entailment-verification layer against MedHallu (I-safety-002a / #924).
2	
3	Codex-designed (.codex/I-safety-002a/codex_medhallu_design.txt). Honest scope: this measures
4	POLARIS's NLI/entailment verifier as a MedHallu detector — NOT the full provenance pipeline
5	(strict_verify cannot run on raw MedHallu answers). Positive class = hallucinated.
6	
7	Frozen knobs (set BEFORE importing the verifier): pinned NLI model, no cross-source, no
8	domain-adaptive, shipped threshold, NO LLM fallback for the headline (abort if NLI down).
9	
10	Usage:
11	  python -m scripts.dr_benchmark.medhallu_runner --split pqa_labeled --limit 10
12	  python -m scripts.dr_benchmark.medhallu_runner --split pqa_labeled --limit 10 --negative-control
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import argparse
18	import asyncio
19	import json
20	import os
21	import random
22	from datetime import datetime, timezone
23	from pathlib import Path
24	
25	# --- Codex: freeze verifier knobs BEFORE importing the NLI verifier (module reads at import) ---
26	os.environ.setdefault("PG_NLI_MODEL", "flan-t5-large")  # pinned, not "auto"
27	os.environ["PG_CROSS_SOURCE_ENABLED"] = "0"             # MedHallu = one gold packet, no corroboration
28	os.environ["PG_NLI_DOMAIN_ADAPTIVE"] = "0"
29	os.environ.setdefault("PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD", "0.65")
30	
31	import pysbd  # noqa: E402
32	
33	from scripts.dr_benchmark.medhallu_adapter import (  # noqa: E402
34	    Confusion,
35	    RunGuards,
36	    add_prediction,
37	    aggregate_answer_verdict,
38	    build_evidence_object,
39	    expected_candidate_count,
40	    metrics,
41	    pair_row,
42	)
43	from src.polaris_graph.agents.nli_verifier import verify_evidence_nli  # noqa: E402
44	
45	_SEG = pysbd.Segmenter(language="en", clean=False)
46	
47	
48	def _join_knowledge(knowledge) -> str:
49	    return " ".join(knowledge) if isinstance(knowledge, list) else str(knowledge)
50	
51	
52	def _split_claims(answer: str) -> list[str]:
53	    return [s.strip() for s in _SEG.segment(answer or "") if s.strip()]
54	
55	
56	async def _verdict_for_candidate(cand, question: str, knowledge: str) -> str:
57	    """Atomize -> claim-by-claim entailment via verify_evidence_nli -> answer verdict.
58	
59	    Aborts the headline (RunGuards) if the NLI model is unavailable (returns []).
60	    """
61	    claims = _split_claims(cand.answer_text)
62	    if not claims:
63	        return "invalid"
64	    evidence = [build_evidence_object(c, question, knowledge, cand) for c in claims]
65	    url_content_map = {ev["source_url"]: knowledge for ev in evidence}
66	    results = await verify_evidence_nli(evidence, url_content_map, research_query="")
67	    if not results:
68	        RunGuards(nli_model_available=False).assert_headline_valid()  # raises
69	    flags = [bool(r.get("is_faithful")) for r in results]
70	    return aggregate_answer_verdict(flags)
71	
72	
73	async def run(split: str, limit: int, negative_control: bool) -> dict:
74	    from datasets import load_dataset
75	
76	    RunGuards(nli_model_available=True, negative_control=negative_control).assert_headline_valid() if not negative_control else None
77	
78	    ds = load_dataset("UTAustin-AIHealth/MedHallu", split, split="train")
79	    n = min(limit, len(ds)) if limit else len(ds)
80	    rows = [ds[i] for i in range(n)]
81	
82	    # Negative control (Codex): shuffle Knowledge across rows; ground-truth support should
83	    # COLLAPSE. If F1 stays high, the detector is using artifacts/memorized labels, not the packet.
84	    if negative_control:
85	        shuffled = [_join_knowledge(r["Knowledge"]) for r in rows]
86	        random.Random(0xC0DEX).shuffle(shuffled)
87	
88	    conf = Confusion()
89	    per_difficulty: dict[str, Confusion] = {}
90	    for i, r in enumerate(rows):
91	        question = r["Question"]
92	        knowledge = shuffled[i] if negative_control else _join_knowledge(r["Knowledge"])
93	        difficulty = str(r.get("Difficulty Level", "unknown"))
94	        row = {
95	            "row_id": f"{split}:{i}",
96	            "question": question,
97	            "knowledge": _join_knowledge(r["Knowledge"]),  # real knowledge for source-isolation guard
98	            "ground_truth": r["Ground Truth"],
99	            "hallucinated_answer": r["Hallucinated Answer"],
100	        }
101	        for cand in pair_row(row, split):
102	            verdict = await _verdict_for_candidate(cand, question, knowledge)
103	            add_prediction(conf, verdict, cand.gold_hallucinated)
104	            d = per_difficulty.setdefault(difficulty, Confusion())
105	            add_prediction(d, verdict, cand.gold_hallucinated)
106	
107	    return {
108	        "split": split,
109	        "rows_scored": n,
110	        "candidates": expected_candidate_count(n, 0)["pqa_labeled"] if split == "pqa_labeled" else n * 2,
111	        "negative_control": negative_control,
112	        "nli_model": os.environ["PG_NLI_MODEL"],
113	        "threshold": float(os.environ["PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD"]),
114	        "overall": metrics(conf),
115	        "by_difficulty": {k: metrics(v) for k, v in sorted(per_difficulty.items())},
116	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
117	        "scope_label": (
118	            "POLARIS entailment-verification layer as a MedHallu detector. Positive class = "
119	            "hallucinated. Verifier: MiniCheck flan-t5-large, threshold 0.65, no retrieval, no "
120	            "strict provenance, no LLM fallback. NOT the full POLARIS provenance pipeline."
121	        ),
122	    }
123	
124	
125	def main() -> None:
126	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
127	    ap.add_argument("--split", default="pqa_labeled", choices=["pqa_labeled", "pqa_artificial"])
128	    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=10)
129	    ap.add_argument("--negative-control", action="store_true")
130	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="")
131	    args = ap.parse_args()
132	
133	    result = asyncio.run(run(args.split, args.limit, args.negative_control))
134	    out = Path(args.out) if args.out else Path(
135	        f"outputs/dr_benchmark/medhallu_{args.split}_n{args.limit}"
136	        f"{'_negctrl' if args.negative_control else ''}.json"
137	    )
138	    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
139	    out.write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
140	    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
141	    print(f"\nwrote {out}")
142	
143	
144	if __name__ == "__main__":
145	    main()
146

1	"""Offline distill-replay proof harness (I-perm-019 / proves #1209 keystone).
2	
3	Controlled A/B over a SAVED drb_76 evidence pool: run the SAME section through
4	the production ``_run_section`` twice — once with ``PG_SECTION_DISTILL`` OFF
5	(legacy single-pass) and once ON (the #1209 map-reduce keystone) — and compare
6	strict_verify-verified sentence counts. Proves the keystone RAISES verified
7	coverage + LOWERS drop rate without new fabrication, BEFORE any paid Q1 re-run.
8	
9	Faithfulness: this harness ORCHESTRATES the real production ``_run_section`` /
10	``strict_verify`` (zero logic drift — the ``tests/polaris_graph/replay``
11	pattern). It changes NO gate. The live A/B makes real LLM calls and is gated
12	behind ``--live`` (default OFF) so this module imports + unit-tests offline.
13	
14	Usage (offline, no spend):
15	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/offline_distill_replay.py            # prints how to run live
16	Usage (live A/B, authorized spend, on the OVH VM):
17	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/offline_distill_replay.py --live \
18	        --pool outputs/audits/I-perm-010/run_drb76_iter2/drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc/evidence_pool.json \
19	        --section-title "Safety and contraindications" \
20	        --section-focus "Safety, adverse events, and contraindications of the intervention" \
21	        --ts 20260611T120000Z
22	
23	LAW II: the verified-count LIFT is a real measured result of the ``--live`` run;
24	this module never fabricates it. Offline it only exercises the counting/compare
25	logic against deterministic inputs.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import argparse
31	import asyncio
32	import json
33	import os
34	import sys
35	from pathlib import Path
36	from typing import Any, Optional
37	
38	# I-perm-019 (#1211) Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: bootstrap the repo root onto
39	# sys.path so the deferred ``import src.polaris_graph...`` in the --live path
40	# resolves when this file is run DIRECTLY (``python
41	# scripts/dr_benchmark/offline_distill_replay.py``) — direct execution seeds
42	# sys.path[0] with scripts/dr_benchmark, NOT the repo root, so without this the
43	# --live arm dies with ``ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'`` before the
44	# A/B proof runs. Mirrors run_honest_sweep_r3.py's ROOT bootstrap.
45	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
46	if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
47	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
48	
49	# Module-level imports of the PRODUCTION generator are deferred into the live
50	# path so the offline unit test can import this module + its pure helpers
51	# without importing the heavy generator stack.
52	
53	
54	def load_pool(path: str | Path) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
55	    """Load a saved evidence_pool.json (a LIST of rows) into the
56	    ``{evidence_id: row}`` dict shape ``_run_section`` consumes. Fail loud on a
57	    row missing ``evidence_id`` (LAW II — no silent drop)."""
58	    raw = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
59	    rows = raw if isinstance(raw, list) else list(raw.values())
60	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
61	    for i, row in enumerate(rows):
62	        eid = row.get("evidence_id")
63	        if not eid:
64	            raise ValueError(f"evidence row {i} has no evidence_id — refusing to drop it silently")
65	        pool[eid] = row
66	    if not pool:
67	        raise ValueError(f"empty evidence pool: {path}")
68	    return pool
69	
70	
71	def result_metrics(res: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
72	    """Extract the comparable metrics from a production ``SectionResult``.
73	
74	    Pure — takes any object exposing the SectionResult fields, so the offline
75	    test can pass a lightweight stand-in.
76	    """
77	    verified = int(getattr(res, "sentences_verified", 0) or 0)
78	    dropped = int(getattr(res, "sentences_dropped", 0) or 0)
79	    total = verified + dropped
80	    verified_text = getattr(res, "verified_text", "") or ""
81	    return {
82	        "sentences_verified": verified,
83	        "sentences_dropped": dropped,
84	        "total_sentences": total,
85	        "drop_rate": (dropped / total) if total else 0.0,
86	        "body_words": len(verified_text.split()),
87	        "regen_attempted": bool(getattr(res, "regen_attempted", False)),
88	        "dropped_due_to_failure": bool(getattr(res, "dropped_due_to_failure", False)),
89	        "input_tokens": int(getattr(res, "input_tokens", 0) or 0),
90	        "output_tokens": int(getattr(res, "output_tokens", 0) or 0),
91	        "error": getattr(res, "error", "") or "",
92	    }
93	
94	
95	def compare(legacy: dict[str, Any], distill: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
96	    """Compare two arms' metrics. ``distill_raises_verified`` is the keystone's
97	    acceptance signal: distilled verified count >= legacy verified count."""
98	    return {
99	        "delta_verified": distill["sentences_verified"] - legacy["sentences_verified"],
100	        "delta_drop_rate": round(distill["drop_rate"] - legacy["drop_rate"], 4),
101	        "delta_body_words": distill["body_words"] - legacy["body_words"],
102	        "distill_raises_verified": distill["sentences_verified"] >= legacy["sentences_verified"],
103	        "distill_lowers_drop_rate": distill["drop_rate"] <= legacy["drop_rate"],
104	    }
105	
106	
107	def build_report(
108	    *,
109	    pool_path: str,
110	    section_title: str,
111	    section_focus: str,
112	    n_ev: int,
113	    model: str,
114	    legacy: dict[str, Any],
115	    distill: dict[str, Any],
116	    ts: str,
117	) -> dict[str, Any]:
118	    """Assemble the full JSON report (pure)."""
119	    return {
120	        "harness": "offline_distill_replay",
121	        "issue": "I-perm-019 / #1209",
122	        "ts": ts,
123	        "pool": pool_path,
124	        "section": {"title": section_title, "focus": section_focus, "n_ev": n_ev},
125	        "model": model,
126	        "legacy_off": legacy,
127	        "distill_on": distill,
128	        "comparison": compare(legacy, distill),
129	    }

131	
132	async def _run_arm(
133	    section: Any,
134	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
135	    *,
136	    distill_on: bool,
137	    model: str,
138	    temperature: float,
139	    max_tokens_per_section: int,
140	    min_kept_fraction: float,
141	) -> Any:
142	    """Run ONE arm through the production ``_run_section`` with the flag toggled.
143	
144	    Sets ``PG_SECTION_DISTILL`` for exactly this call and restores it after, so
145	    the two arms are otherwise identical inputs.
146	    """
147	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import _run_section
148	
149	    prev = os.environ.get("PG_SECTION_DISTILL")
150	    os.environ["PG_SECTION_DISTILL"] = "1" if distill_on else "0"
151	    try:
152	        return await _run_section(
153	            section,
154	            pool,
155	            model=model,
156	            temperature=temperature,
157	            max_tokens_per_section=max_tokens_per_section,
158	            min_kept_fraction=min_kept_fraction,
159	        )
160	    finally:
161	        if prev is None:
162	            os.environ.pop("PG_SECTION_DISTILL", None)
163	        else:
164	            os.environ["PG_SECTION_DISTILL"] = prev
165	
166	
167	async def run_live_ab(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
168	    """Run both arms live, write the report + per-arm verified text, and return
169	    a process exit code (nonzero if the keystone did NOT raise verified count —
170	    fail loud, never pass a regression silently)."""
171	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan
172	
173	    pool = load_pool(args.pool)
174	    ev_ids = list(pool.keys())
175	    if args.max_ev and args.max_ev > 0:
176	        ev_ids = ev_ids[: args.max_ev]
177	    section = SectionPlan(title=args.section_title, focus=args.section_focus, ev_ids=ev_ids)
178	
179	    legacy_res = await _run_arm(
180	        section, pool, distill_on=False, model=args.model,
181	        temperature=args.temperature, max_tokens_per_section=args.max_tokens,
182	        min_kept_fraction=args.min_kept_fraction,
183	    )
184	    distill_res = await _run_arm(
185	        section, pool, distill_on=True, model=args.model,
186	        temperature=args.temperature, max_tokens_per_section=args.max_tokens,
187	        min_kept_fraction=args.min_kept_fraction,
188	    )
189	
190	    legacy = result_metrics(legacy_res)
191	    distill = result_metrics(distill_res)
192	    report = build_report(
193	        pool_path=args.pool, section_title=args.section_title,
194	        section_focus=args.section_focus, n_ev=len(ev_ids), model=args.model,
195	        legacy=legacy, distill=distill, ts=args.ts,
196	    )
197	
198	    out_dir = Path(args.out or "outputs/audits/I-perm-019")
199	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
200	    (out_dir / f"replay_{args.ts}.json").write_text(
201	        json.dumps(report, indent=2), encoding="utf-8"
202	    )
203	    # Dump both arms' verified prose for the §-1.1 line-by-line fabrication audit.
204	    (out_dir / f"legacy_{args.ts}.txt").write_text(
205	        getattr(legacy_res, "verified_text", "") or "", encoding="utf-8"
206	    )
207	    (out_dir / f"distill_{args.ts}.txt").write_text(
208	        getattr(distill_res, "verified_text", "") or "", encoding="utf-8"
209	    )
210	
211	    cmp = report["comparison"]
212	    print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
213	    print(
214	        f"\n[offline_distill_replay] verified: legacy={legacy['sentences_verified']} "
215	        f"-> distill={distill['sentences_verified']} (delta {cmp['delta_verified']:+d}); "
216	        f"drop_rate {legacy['drop_rate']:.2f} -> {distill['drop_rate']:.2f}; "
217	        f"body_words {legacy['body_words']} -> {distill['body_words']}"
218	    )
219	    if not cmp["distill_raises_verified"]:
220	        print(
221	            "[offline_distill_replay] FAIL: distill LOWERED verified count — "
222	            "the keystone regressed on this section. Investigate before any paid run."
223	        )
224	        return 1
225	    print("[offline_distill_replay] PASS: distill did not lower verified count.")
226	    return 0

229	def build_arg_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
230	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Offline distill-replay A/B proof harness (#1209).")
231	    p.add_argument("--live", action="store_true",
232	                   help="Make REAL LLM calls (authorized spend). Default OFF refuses to run the arms.")
233	    p.add_argument("--pool", type=str,
234	                   default="outputs/audits/I-perm-010/run_drb76_iter2/drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc/evidence_pool.json")
235	    p.add_argument("--section-title", type=str, default="Safety and contraindications")
236	    p.add_argument("--section-focus", type=str,

238	    p.add_argument("--max-ev", type=int, default=40,
239	                   help="Cap evidence rows fed to the section (mirrors PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION).")
240	    p.add_argument("--model", type=str, default=os.getenv("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"))
241	    p.add_argument("--temperature", type=float, default=0.3)

243	    p.add_argument("--min-kept-fraction", type=float, default=0.4)
244	    p.add_argument("--ts", type=str, default="manual",
245	                   help="Timestamp tag for output filenames (pass explicitly; no wall-clock in-script).")
246	    p.add_argument("--out", type=str, default="")
247	    return p
248	
249	
250	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
251	    args = build_arg_parser().parse_args(argv)
252	    if not args.live:
253	        print(
254	            "offline_distill_replay: refusing to run the LIVE A/B without --live "
255	            "(it makes real, billable LLM calls).\n"
256	            "To run the authorized micro-proof on the OVH VM:\n"
257	            "  python scripts/dr_benchmark/offline_distill_replay.py --live \\\n"
258	            f"    --pool {build_arg_parser().get_default('pool')} \\\n"
259	            "    --section-title \"Safety and contraindications\" \\\n"
260	            "    --section-focus \"Safety, adverse events, and contraindications of the intervention\" \\\n"
261	            "    --ts <UTC_STAMP>\n"
262	            "It runs the SAME section through _run_section with PG_SECTION_DISTILL OFF then ON and "
263	            "compares strict_verify-verified sentence counts."
264	        )
265	        return 0
266	    return asyncio.run(run_live_ab(args))
267	
268	
269	if __name__ == "__main__":
270	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	"""Offline no-spend END-TO-END harness (I-meta-002 PR-9 / readiness item 9).
2	
3	ONE offline proof that the WHOLE DR toolchain runs end to end so canary day adds ONLY real
4	model calls. NO MONEY, NO NETWORK anywhere: zero real LLM calls (the generator AND the three
5	verifier roles are faked / canned), zero socket. Codex DESIGN APPROVE iter 2
6	(.codex/I-meta-002-pr9-e2e/design_brief.md; zero P0/P1; the 3 P2s are folded in here +
7	enforced by the driving test).
8	
9	The chain this harness exercises (all offline):
10	
11	  A. 4-ROLE SEAM (M3a builder + M3b seam + M5 evaluator_agrees) over the REAL annotated
12	     `clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm` contract, fed CANNED kept verified sentences + a canned
13	     evidence pool through an INJECTED FAKE `RoleTransport` (no httpx, no socket). Produces the
14	     manifest `four_role_evaluation` block (final_verdicts + held/coverage) PLUS the M5
15	     `evaluator_agrees` map (built by `sweep_integration.build_evaluator_agrees_map`, the SINGLE
16	     source of the §-1.1 safe rule — never reimplemented here) and the
17	     `four_role_claim_audit.json` the seam writes next to the run.
18	
19	  B. M4 PATH-B served==pinned gate over FIXTURE self-host served-metadata: preflight (offline)
20	     + assert_post_run on a MATCHING `{model, endpoint}` -> PASS; and on a WRONG-MODEL fixture
21	     -> fail-closed (`GateError`). NO OpenRouter resolution (self-host serving_route branch),
22	     NO socket (offline=True everywhere).
23	
24	  C. EXTERNAL SCORER on SYNTHETIC, ISOLATED fixtures (Codex P2 #1): two single-auditor ledgers
25	     (claude + codex) -> `reconcile` (conservative-MAX) -> a reconciled ledger ->
26	     `score_run.score_one` -> a per-claim scored JSON -> `aggregate_systems` -> a systems
27	     summary. The fixtures live under `tests/fixtures/offline_e2e/` and are clearly labeled
28	     synthetic; this leg NEVER reads or writes under `outputs/dr_benchmark/`.
29	
30	IMPORT-SAFE: importing this module performs NO I/O, opens NO socket, and starts NO subprocess.
31	Every function below is pure orchestration over caller-supplied paths / an injected transport;
32	all real work happens only when a function is called (the driving test calls them with a
33	`tmp_path` and a fake transport). There is no `__main__` runtime path that spends or networks.
34	
35	CONTAMINATION-CRITICAL (§-1.1, operator-locked): leg A uses ONLY the native scope contract;
36	leg C uses ONLY the synthetic isolated fixtures. NOTHING here reads `outputs/dr_benchmark/`.
37	"""

111	# Benchmark routing — resolve a slug -> its SWEEP_QUERIES domain -> its native contract.
112	# This is the WIRING proof: the same path production uses (SWEEP_QUERIES registration -> the
113	# question's domain -> load_scope_template(domain) -> load_required_entities(template, slug)).

115	def lookup_benchmark_query(slug: str) -> dict:
116	    """Return the SWEEP_QUERIES registration entry for `slug` (fail closed if unregistered).
117	
118	    Imported LAZILY (inside the call) so this module stays import-safe: importing
119	    `scripts.dr_benchmark.offline_e2e` performs NO I/O and pulls no big sweep file at module
120	    load. A slug that is not registered — or a registration that lost its `domain` — fails LOUD
121	    here (a missing/typo'd registration must fail the wiring proof, never silently default).
122	    """
123	    from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import SWEEP_QUERIES
124	
125	    matches = [q for q in SWEEP_QUERIES if q.get("slug") == slug]
126	    if not matches:
127	        raise ValueError(
128	            f"lookup_benchmark_query: slug {slug!r} is not registered in SWEEP_QUERIES; the "
129	            f"4-role wiring proof requires the question to be routed by its registration "
130	            f"(fail-closed, no hardcoded slug->domain fallback)."
131	        )
132	    if len(matches) > 1:
133	        raise ValueError(
134	            f"lookup_benchmark_query: slug {slug!r} is registered {len(matches)} times in "
135	            f"SWEEP_QUERIES; a benchmark slug must route to exactly one domain (fail-closed)."
136	        )
137	    entry = matches[0]
138	    if not entry.get("domain"):
139	        raise ValueError(
140	            f"lookup_benchmark_query: SWEEP_QUERIES entry for slug {slug!r} has no domain; the "
141	            f"4-role builder keys the frozen contract by (domain template, slug) — a domain-less "
142	            f"registration cannot route (fail-closed)."
143	        )
144	    return entry
145	
146	
147	def resolve_benchmark_required_element_ids(slug: str) -> list[str]:
148	    """Resolve `slug`'s native required-element ids through the FULL production routing path.
149	
150	    SWEEP_QUERIES[slug].domain -> `load_scope_template(domain)` (scope_gate, the same loader the
151	    sweep uses) -> `native_gate_b_inputs.load_required_entities(template, slug)`. Returns the
152	    ordered entity-id list (the coverage DENOMINATOR for that question). NON-EMPTY by contract
153	    (`load_required_entities` raises on a missing/empty contract). NO network, NO spend — pure
154	    YAML reads. This is the routing leg (build-spec item 1): the question's registered domain
155	    must load the template that actually holds that slug's contract.
156	    """
157	    entry = lookup_benchmark_query(slug)
158	    template = load_scope_template(entry["domain"])
159	    entities = load_required_entities(template, slug)
160	    return [entity["id"] for entity in entities]

163	def build_benchmark_denominator(slug: str) -> list[str]:

165	
166	    Drives `make_gate_b_input_builder()` (the exact closure `run_gate_b` wires into the sweep)
167	    with the shared canned report + ev_pool over THAT slug's domain-routed native contract, and
168	    returns `bundle.inputs.coverage_ledger.required_element_ids`. This is build-spec item 2 (the
169	    robustness core): it proves the M3a builder, given THIS question, builds the denominator from
170	    THIS slug's contract — not from a hardcoded value and not from another question's contract.
171	    NO network, NO spend (the builder is a pure function; no transport is invoked here).
172	    """
173	    entry = lookup_benchmark_query(slug)
174	    domain = entry["domain"]
175	    template = load_scope_template(domain)
176	    builder = make_gate_b_input_builder()
177	    bundle = builder(
178	        multi=build_canned_report(),
179	        template=template,
180	        slug=slug,
181	        domain=domain,
182	        ev_pool=build_canned_ev_pool(),
183	    )
184	    return list(bundle.inputs.coverage_ledger.required_element_ids)

187	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
188	# Leg A — the canned FAKE RoleTransport + in-memory report objects (NO network, NO spend).
189	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
190	class PerClaimFakeRoleTransport:
191	    """Canned in-process `RoleTransport` — NO network, NO spend (reuses the proven
192	    tests/dr_benchmark/test_gate_b_seam.py pattern, extended to PER-CLAIM judge verdicts).
193	
194	    Mirror pass-1 cites the FIRST supplied evidence doc_id (so the grounding binding holds for
195	    whatever evidence_id the builder minted); pass-2 echoes the embedded content_hash. Sentinel
196	    returns GROUNDED (`<score>no</score>`). Judge returns FABRICATED for any claim whose prompt
197	    carries `FABRICATED_CLAIM_MARKER`, else VERIFIED — so the SAME run produces both an
198	    evaluator_agrees=True (VERIFIED + kept) and an evaluator_agrees=False (FABRICATED) entry,
199	    which is the §-1.1 safe-rule property the E2E asserts. `completions` counts in-process
200	    completions (NEVER an HTTP POST) so the test can assert the verifier roles actually ran.
201	    """
202	
203	    def __init__(self) -> None:
204	        self.completions = 0  # canned in-process completions (NEVER an HTTP POST).
205	
206	    def complete(self, request: RoleRequest) -> RoleResponse:
207	        self.completions += 1
208	        if request.role == "mirror":
209	            if "pass2_input" in (request.params or {}):
210	                content_hash = request.params["pass2_input"]["content_hash"]
211	                payload = {"content_hash": content_hash, "classification": "supported"}
212	                return RoleResponse(raw_text=json.dumps(payload), served_model=request.model_slug)
213	            documents = (request.params or {}).get("documents") or []
214	            doc_id = documents[0]["doc_id"] if documents else "doc0"
215	            return RoleResponse(
216	                raw_text="grounded answer",
217	                served_model=request.model_slug,
218	                citations=[CitationSpan(span_start=0, span_end=8, doc_ids=(doc_id,))],
219	            )
220	        if request.role == "sentinel":
221	            # GROUNDED in whichever groundedness mode the adapter resolved (I-run11-002 L1 +
222	            # I-run11-004): decomposition (MiniMax-M2 default) -> JSON {"verdict": "supported"};
223	            # guardian -> `<score>no</score>` (no risk => grounded, lethal-polarity yes=risk);
224	            # noninverted -> one-word GROUNDED.
225	            final_instruction = request.messages[-1]["content"] if request.messages else ""
226	            if "Decompose the CLAIM into atomic sub-assertions" in final_instruction:
227	                # Full decomposition contract (I-run11-004 brief-gate P1): a "supported" verdict needs
228	                # a non-empty atoms list + unsupported_atoms, else the parser fails closed.
229	                sentinel_raw = ('{"verdict": "supported", "unsupported_atoms": 0, '
230	                                '"atoms": [{"atom": "x", "type": "mechanism", "status": "supported"}]}')
231	            elif "<guardian>" in final_instruction:
232	                sentinel_raw = "<score>no</score>"
233	            else:
234	                sentinel_raw = "GROUNDED"
235	            return RoleResponse(raw_text=sentinel_raw, served_model=request.model_slug)
236	        if request.role == "judge":
237	            verdict = (
238	                JUDGE_FABRICATED
239	                if FABRICATED_CLAIM_MARKER in (request.prompt or "")
240	                else JUDGE_VERIFIED
241	            )
242	            return RoleResponse(raw_text=verdict, served_model=request.model_slug)
243	        raise AssertionError(f"unexpected role {request.role!r}")
244

270	def build_canned_report() -> _FakeMulti:
271	    """A finished report (two KEPT verified sentences) that cites the canned evidence pool.
272	
273	    Sentence 1 cites SURPASS-2's REAL DOI (an annotated S1 trial entity in the tirzepatide
274	    contract) -> covers that element on a VERIFIED Judge verdict -> evaluator_agrees True.
275	    Sentence 2 carries `FABRICATED_CLAIM_MARKER` -> the fake Judge returns FABRICATED -> final
276	    verdict FABRICATED -> evaluator_agrees False. Both are KEPT (is_verified=True), so the
277	    evaluator_agrees boolean is driven purely by the verdict, exactly as the sweep path does.
278	    """
279	    return _FakeMulti(
280	        sections=[
281	            _FakeSection(
282	                "Efficacy",
283	                [
284	                    _FakeVerification(
285	                        "SURPASS-2 randomized 1879 patients; tirzepatide lowered HbA1c.",
286	                        [_FakeToken("ev_000")],
287	                    )
288	                ],
289	            ),
290	            _FakeSection(
291	                "Safety",
292	                [
293	                    _FakeVerification(
294	                        f"{FABRICATED_CLAIM_MARKER} An unsupported safety claim with no "
295	                        "grounded evidence backing.",
296	                        [_FakeToken("ev_001")],
297	                    )
298	                ],
299	            ),
300	        ]
301	    )

304	def build_canned_ev_pool() -> dict:
305	    """Raw evidence-pool rows (the run's ev_pool shape) the canned report cites.
306	
307	    `ev_000` carries SURPASS-2's real DOI via a journal URL so the M3a coverage matcher's EXACT
308	    canonical-identifier equality credits the trial element. `ev_001` is a generic source for
309	    the FABRICATED sentence (it never earns coverage because its final verdict is FABRICATED).
310	    """
311	    return {
312	        "ev_000": {
313	            "evidence_id": "ev_000",
314	            "direct_quote": "SURPASS-2 randomized 1879 patients; tirzepatide lowered HbA1c.",
315	            "source_url": "https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2107519",
316	        },
317	        "ev_001": {
318	            "evidence_id": "ev_001",
319	            "direct_quote": "A generic source paragraph cited by the unsupported safety claim.",
320	            "source_url": "https://example.test/generic-source",
321	        },
322	    }

325	@dataclass
326	class FourRoleLegResult:
327	    """Leg-A output: the assembled manifest plus the seam result + the parsed audit map."""
328	
329	    manifest: dict
330	    result: FourRoleEvaluationResult
331	    audit: dict
332	
333	
334	def run_four_role_leg(
335	    transport: PerClaimFakeRoleTransport,
336	    *,
337	    run_dir: Path,
338	    timestamp: str = DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP,
339	    domain: str = TIRZEPATIDE_DOMAIN,
340	    slug: str = TIRZEPATIDE_SLUG,
341	) -> FourRoleLegResult:
342	    """Leg A: run the REAL M3a/M3b 4-role seam offline over a NATIVE scope contract.
343	
344	    Resolves the template through the SAME production loader the sweep uses
345	    (`scope_gate.load_scope_template(domain)`) — NOT a hardcoded file path — so this leg proves
346	    the WIRING (the question's domain loads the template that holds its slug's contract), then
347	    reuses the production builder closure (`make_gate_b_input_builder`) + `run_four_role_seam`
348	    with the INJECTED fake transport and the shared canned report/ev_pool. Defaults route the
349	    annotated non-benchmark tirzepatide contract (clinical); the 5 benchmark questions pass their
350	    own (domain, slug). Then assembles the manifest `four_role_evaluation` block EXACTLY as
351	    `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` does (including the M5 `evaluator_agrees` map via
352	    `build_evaluator_agrees_map`), and reads back the `four_role_claim_audit.json` the seam wrote
353	    next to the run. NO network, NO spend.
354	    """
355	    template = load_scope_template(domain)
356	    builder = make_gate_b_input_builder()
357	    result = run_four_role_seam(
358	        transport,
359	        run_dir=run_dir,
360	        timestamp=timestamp,
361	        four_role_input_builder=builder,
362	        multi=build_canned_report(),
363	        template=template,
364	        slug=slug,
365	        domain=domain,
366	        ev_pool=build_canned_ev_pool(),
367	    )
368	
369	    # Assemble the manifest block exactly as the sweep does (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:3206-3235).
370	    # The M5 evaluator_agrees map uses build_evaluator_agrees_map — the SINGLE source of the
371	    # §-1.1 safe rule (VERIFIED + kept -> True; every other verdict -> False). kept_claim_ids is
372	    # None here for the same reason as the sweep: every claim_id in final_verdicts was built from
373	    # a KEPT (is_verified) sentence by the M3a builder.
374	    manifest: dict = {}
375	    manifest["four_role_evaluation"] = {
376	        "release_allowed": result.release_allowed,
377	        "held_reasons": result.held_reasons,
378	        "coverage_fraction": round(result.coverage_fraction, 3),
379	        "fabricated_occurrence_latched": result.fabricated_occurrence_latched,
380	        "final_verdicts": dict(result.final_verdicts),
381	    }
382	    manifest["four_role_evaluation"]["evaluator_agrees"] = build_evaluator_agrees_map(
383	        result.final_verdicts
384	    )
385	
386	    audit_path = run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_AUDIT_FILENAME
387	    audit = json.loads(audit_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
388	    return FourRoleLegResult(manifest=manifest, result=result, audit=audit)

395	    """A self-host RolePin (surrogate_fields unused by the self-host branch but the preflight

400	def run_m4_gate_pass(
401	    *,
402	    salt: bytes,
403	    role: str = "mirror",
404	    slug: str = MIRROR_SLUG,
405	    base_url: str = SELF_HOST_BASE_URL,
406	) -> dict:
407	    """Leg B (PASS): preflight (offline) + assert_post_run on a MATCHING self-host served-meta.
408	
409	    NO network: offline=True takes the self-host serving_route branch (no OpenRouter
410	    resolution); the served `{model, endpoint}` matches the pinned slug + base_url, so the gate
411	    returns the established per-role served-identity surrogates. `enforce_architecture_coverage`
412	    is False (offline test mode uses a single self-host pin, not the full 4-role architecture).
413	    The caller must set `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL` in the env (the test does so via monkeypatch).
414	    """
415	    pins = [_self_host_pin(role, slug)]
416	    pin = preflight([], pins, salt, offline=True, enforce_architecture_coverage=False)
417	    # Served endpoint reported WITH a trailing slash — must still match (trailing-slash tolerant).
418	    calls = [
419	        LLMCall(
420	            call_id="offline-e2e-mirror",
421	            role=role,
422	            prompt_messages_present=True,
423	            request_hash="offline-e2e-request-hash",
424	            response_metadata={"model": slug, "endpoint": base_url + "/"},
425	        )
426	    ]
427	    return assert_post_run(pin, [], salt, calls, {"serper", "semantic_scholar"})

431	    *,
432	    role: str = "mirror",
433	    wrong_slug: str = "cohere/command-r-plus",
434	    base_url: str = SELF_HOST_BASE_URL,
435	) -> tuple[list, LLMCall]:
436	    """Leg B (FAIL-CLOSED): build the self-host pin + a WRONG-MODEL served-meta LLMCall.
437	
438	    Returns `(pins, wrong_call)`; the test runs preflight + assert_post_run with these and
439	    asserts a `GateError` (a wrong verifier model is a silent capability downgrade — must abort).
440	    """
