442	    wrong_call = LLMCall(
443	        call_id="offline-e2e-mirror-wrong",
444	        role=role,
445	        prompt_messages_present=True,
446	        request_hash="offline-e2e-request-hash",
447	        response_metadata={"model": wrong_slug, "endpoint": base_url},
448	    )
449	    return pins, wrong_call
450	
451	
452	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
453	# Leg C — external scorer over SYNTHETIC, ISOLATED fixtures (NEVER outputs/dr_benchmark).
454	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
455	# Repo-relative location of the synthetic, isolated scorer fixtures (Codex P2 #1).
456	OFFLINE_E2E_FIXTURE_DIR = Path("tests/fixtures/offline_e2e")
457	SYNTHETIC_RUBRIC_NAME = "synthetic_rubric.json"
458	SYNTHETIC_LEDGER_CLAUDE_NAME = "synthetic_ledger_claude.json"
459	SYNTHETIC_LEDGER_CODEX_NAME = "synthetic_ledger_codex.json"
460	
461	# The synthetic fixtures are tagged (system, question). Kept here so the harness and the test
462	# read the same identity (and never a real benchmark question's gold data).
463	SYNTHETIC_SYSTEM = "chatgpt"
464	SYNTHETIC_QUESTION_ID = "Q75"
465	
466	
467	@dataclass
468	class ScorerLegResult:
469	    """Leg-C output: the paths written under the caller-supplied tmp out-dir + the scored dict."""
470	
471	    reconciled_ledger_path: Path
472	    scored_json_path: Path
473	    systems_summary_path: Path
474	    scored: dict
475	
476	
477	def run_external_scorer_leg(
478	    *,
479	    out_dir: Path,
480	    fixture_dir: Path = OFFLINE_E2E_FIXTURE_DIR,
481	) -> ScorerLegResult:
482	    """Leg C: reconcile two synthetic single-auditor ledgers -> score_one -> aggregate_systems.
483	
484	    Reads ONLY the synthetic, isolated fixtures under `fixture_dir` (NEVER
485	    `outputs/dr_benchmark/`). Writes the reconciled ledger, the scored JSON, and the systems
486	    summary ONLY under the caller-supplied `out_dir` (NEVER `outputs/dr_benchmark/`). Pure
487	    offline logic + JSON/markdown writes; no network, no spend.
488	
489	    `score_one` requires `auditor == "reconciled"`, which only `reconcile()` produces — so this
490	    leg exercises the real dual-§-1.1 conservative-MAX reconciliation before scoring.
491	    """
492	    # aggregate_systems is imported lazily so this module's top-level import stays minimal /
493	    # side-effect-free (it is only needed when this leg actually runs).
494	    from scripts.dr_benchmark.aggregate_systems import render_final_report
495	
496	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
497	    rubric_path = fixture_dir / SYNTHETIC_RUBRIC_NAME
498	    claude = load_ledger(fixture_dir / SYNTHETIC_LEDGER_CLAUDE_NAME)
499	    codex = load_ledger(fixture_dir / SYNTHETIC_LEDGER_CODEX_NAME)
500	
501	    reconciled = reconcile(claude, codex)
502	    reconciled_path = out_dir / "reconciled_ledger.json"
503	    dump_ledger(reconciled, reconciled_path)
504	
505	    scored = score_one(
506	        system=SYNTHETIC_SYSTEM,
507	        question_id=SYNTHETIC_QUESTION_ID,
508	        rubric_path=rubric_path,
509	        ledger_path=reconciled_path,
510	    )
511	    # score_run.main writes <system>_<question>.json into a scored dir; mirror that name so
512	    # aggregate_systems._collect picks it up.
513	    scored_dir = out_dir / "scored"
514	    scored_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
515	    scored_json_path = scored_dir / f"{SYNTHETIC_SYSTEM}_{SYNTHETIC_QUESTION_ID}.json"
516	    scored_json_path.write_text(
517	        json.dumps(scored, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
518	        encoding="utf-8",
519	    )
520	
521	    systems_summary_path = out_dir / "systems_summary.md"
522	    # freeze_pin points at a non-existent path under out_dir so the aggregator renders the
523	    # "IDENTITY UNVERIFIED" branch (this is a SYNTHETIC dry-run summary, not a real report) and
524	    # never reads anything under outputs/dr_benchmark.
525	    render_final_report(
526	        scored_dir=scored_dir,
527	        freeze_pin=out_dir / "synthetic_freeze_pin_absent.txt",
528	        out_path=systems_summary_path,
529	    )
530	    return ScorerLegResult(
531	        reconciled_ledger_path=reconciled_path,
532	        scored_json_path=scored_json_path,
533	        systems_summary_path=systems_summary_path,
534	        scored=scored,
535	    )
536

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Pack a POLARIS report into the official DeepTRACE (answer-engine-eval) input schema:
3	one record per task = {id, Question, Output, S1..S10}. The official scorer
4	(third_party/answer-engine-eval/Venkit.et.al.2024/populate_scores.py) then scrapes each
5	S{i} via r.jina.ai, gpt-4o-extracts core statements, and judges (statement, source) support.
6	
7	THE 10-SOURCE CAP (code-confirmed: populate_scores.py uses `range(1,11)` x3; the body's `[N]`
8	markers are parsed by utils_misc.extract_citations as source indices N). A POLARIS report
9	cites hundreds of sources, so this packer reduces the body to a MINIMAL load-bearing set of
10	<=10 sources via the harness's OWN greedy_set_cover objective (DeepTRACE source-necessity #6),
11	and RENUMBERS the body citations to `[1..k]`, k<=10. Statements whose only sources are dropped
12	become uncited (honest — we do not invent support). The full keep-all corroboration set is NOT
13	listed here (it lives in POLARIS's own bibliography/sidecar, scored by DRB-II independence).
14	
15	INVARIANTS (eval integrity):
16	  - NEVER pre-fills S{i}_content (the official scorer scrapes it; pre-filling POLARIS's own
17	    spans would be the §-1.1 self-serving-eval shortcut — forbidden).
18	  - Output is the cleaned answer body (masthead/base64/gap-stub stripped via pack_drb2.strip_junk,
19	    [#ev:] provenance tokens removed, appendix dropped) — never POLARIS-internal meta.
20	  - Faithfulness untouched: this packs/renumbers existing citations; it judges nothing.
21	LAW VI: caps are env-overridable.
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import argparse
26	import json
27	import os
28	import re
29	import sys
30	from collections import defaultdict
31	from pathlib import Path
32	
33	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
34	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO / "scripts" / "dr_benchmark"))
35	import pack_drb2  # noqa: E402  (reuse strip_junk / answer_body)
36	
37	MAX_SOURCES = int(os.getenv("PACK_DEEPTRACE_MAX_SOURCES", "10"))  # the official range(1,11) cap
38	
39	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[([\d,\s]+)\]")          # [N] / [N, M] numbered citations
40	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")      # POLARIS-internal provenance tokens (stripped)
41	# Intra-line sentence boundary (applied AFTER the body is split into lines): POLARIS's
42	# trailing-citation prose style `... end.[1] Next ...[2]`. Fixed-width lookbehind (1-char class).
43	_INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?\]])\s+(?=[A-Z0-9])")
44	
45	
46	def _split_statements(body: str) -> list[str]:
47	    """Split a report body into the FINEST citation-bearing units for the source-necessity
48	    matrix. Each Markdown LINE is its own block, so table rows / list items / headings / block
49	    quotes never collapse into one another (the Codex P1 class — first lists, then tables); a
50	    table row (`|`-led) is further split per-cell on `|` so two cells citing two sources stay
51	    distinct; a prose line is split into sentences on terminal punctuation.
52	
53	    DIRECTIONAL SAFETY: over-splitting is harmless for greedy set-cover (a source that uniquely
54	    covers any unit is still selected — it never DROPS a load-bearing source); only UNDER-splitting
55	    (collapsing distinct cited units) drops sources. This matrix is INTERNAL source-selection only;
56	    the official scorer re-extracts statements from `Output` itself, so granularity here never
57	    changes what the judge reads — it only makes the <=10 kept sources strictly more conservative."""
58	    statements: list[str] = []
59	    for line in body.splitlines():
60	        s = line.strip()
61	        if not s:
62	            continue
63	        units = [c for c in s.split("|") if c.strip()] if s.startswith("|") else [s]
64	        for unit in units:
65	            for seg in _INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE.split(unit):
66	                if seg.strip():
67	                    statements.append(seg)
68	    return statements
69	
70	
71	def _load_references(bibliography: list) -> dict[int, str]:
72	    """Map the citation number `num` -> source url from a POLARIS bibliography.json (a list of
73	    {num, url, ...} entries). Skips malformed entries (no num/url)."""
74	    refs: dict[int, str] = {}
75	    for entry in bibliography:
76	        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
77	            continue
78	        num, url = entry.get("num"), entry.get("url")
79	        if num is None or not url:
80	            continue
81	        try:
82	            refs[int(num)] = str(url)
83	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
84	            continue
85	    return refs
86	
87	
88	def _citation_matrix(sentences: list[str]) -> dict[int, set[int]]:
89	    """source_num -> set of sentence indices that cite it (the CITATION bipartite)."""
90	    m: dict[int, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
91	    for i, s in enumerate(sentences):
92	        for grp in _CITE_RE.findall(s):
93	            for tok in grp.split(","):
94	                tok = tok.strip()
95	                if tok.isdigit():
96	                    m[int(tok)].add(i)
97	    return m
98	
99	
100	def _greedy_set_cover(universe: set[int], subsets: dict[int, set[int]]) -> list[int]:
101	    """Greedy set cover (mirrors third_party/answer-engine-eval utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover —
102	    the SAME objective the DeepTRACE source-necessity metric scores). Returns the source nums in
103	    coverage-priority order; deterministic tie-break by (-new_coverage, num)."""
104	    covered: set[int] = set()
105	    cover: list[int] = []
106	    items = list(subsets.items())
107	    while covered != universe and items:
108	        num, subset = max(items, key=lambda kv: (len(kv[1] - covered), -kv[0]))
109	        gain = len(subset - covered)
110	        if gain == 0:
111	            break
112	        cover.append(num)
113	        covered |= subset
114	        items = [(n, s) for n, s in items if n != num]
115	    return cover
116	
117	
118	def _renumber_body(body: str, num_to_new: dict[int, int]) -> str:
119	    """Rewrite `[old]`/`[old, ...]` -> `[new]` for selected sources; DROP citations to
120	    non-selected sources (the statement becomes uncited — never invent support)."""
121	    def repl(m: re.Match) -> str:
122	        new = sorted({num_to_new[int(t.strip())]
123	                      for t in m.group(1).split(",")
124	                      if t.strip().isdigit() and int(t.strip()) in num_to_new})
125	        return "[" + ", ".join(str(n) for n in new) + "]" if new else ""
126	    return _CITE_RE.sub(repl, body)
127	
128	
129	def pack_report_for_deeptrace(
130	    report_md: str, bibliography: list, *, task_id: object, question: str,
131	    max_sources: int = MAX_SOURCES,
132	) -> dict:
133	    """Return one DeepTRACE record {id, Question, Output, S1..S10} for a POLARIS report."""
134	    refs = _load_references(bibliography)
135	    # clean body: strip masthead/base64/gap-stub + appendix + provenance tokens.
136	    cleaned, _ = pack_drb2.strip_junk(report_md)
137	    body = pack_drb2.answer_body(cleaned)
138	    body = _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", body)
139	    sentences = _split_statements(body)
140	    matrix = {n: idxs for n, idxs in _citation_matrix(sentences).items() if n in refs}
141	    universe = set().union(*matrix.values()) if matrix else set()
142	    # Clamp the selectable cap to 1..10 — the official scorer dereferences exactly S1..S10
143	    # (range(1,11)); a cap <10 would KeyError, >10 would emit out-of-range sources (Codex P1).
144	    n_cap = max(1, min(10, max_sources))
145	    cover = _greedy_set_cover(universe, matrix)[:n_cap]
146	    num_to_new = {old: i + 1 for i, old in enumerate(cover)}
147	    out_body = _renumber_body(body, num_to_new)
148	    record = {"id": str(task_id), "Question": question, "Output": out_body}
149	    for i in range(1, 11):  # ALWAYS emit exactly S1..S10 (empty string when unfilled).
150	        record[f"S{i}"] = refs.get(cover[i - 1], "") if i - 1 < len(cover) else ""
151	        # NB: S{i}_content is DELIBERATELY NOT set — the official scorer scrapes it via jina.
152	    return record
153	
154	
155	def main() -> None:
156	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Pack a POLARIS report for DeepTRACE (answer-engine-eval).")
157	    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True)
158	    ap.add_argument("--bibliography", required=True)
159	    ap.add_argument("--task-id", required=True)
160	    ap.add_argument("--question", required=True)
161	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True, help="output JSON path (a list of one record, appended)")
162	    args = ap.parse_args()
163	    bib = json.loads(Path(args.bibliography).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
164	    rec = pack_report_for_deeptrace(
165	        Path(args.report).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"), bib,
166	        task_id=args.task_id, question=args.question,
167	    )
168	    out = Path(args.out)
169	    data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if out.is_file() else []
170	    data = [d for d in data if d.get("id") != rec["id"]] + [rec]
171	    out.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
172	    # The record ALWAYS has exactly S1..S10 (the helper clamps the cap to 1..10); count over the
173	    # real schema, NOT the raw env cap — PACK_DEEPTRACE_MAX_SOURCES=99 would KeyError on S11 (Codex P1).
174	    n_src = sum(1 for i in range(1, 11) if rec[f"S{i}"])
175	    print(f"[pack_deeptrace] wrote {out} | id={rec['id']} | listed_sources={n_src} (<= 10)")
176	
177	
178	if __name__ == "__main__":
179	    main()
180

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Pack a POLARIS run's report.md into the official DeepResearch-Bench-II submission
3	layout (report/<model>/idx-<task>.md) so the official scorer (third_party/
4	DeepResearch-Bench-II/run_evaluation.py, Gemini rubric judge) can score it.
5	
6	The DRB-II scorer reads the markdown and truncates at MAX_PAPER_CHARS (=150000 per the
7	official .env_example); the run-#7 masthead-swallow (TI-22: a source masthead promoted to
8	a `# ` H1 + a ~95k-char swallowed line) silently consumed ~74% of the budget. This packer
9	removes ONLY provably-non-answer junk and FAILS LOUD on silent content loss:
10	
11	  - strips foreign source-masthead `# ` H1s (the report's own structure is `##`/`###`),
12	  - strips oversized swallowed lines (> PACK_DRB2_MAX_LINE_CHARS),
13	  - strips base64 / data-URI image payloads,
14	  - omits gap-stub META-TEXT (placeholder prose for already-dropped claims — not answer
15	    content; DeepTRACE/DRB-II would score it as on-topic-but-non-substantive),
16	  - FAILS LOUD (SystemExit) if the ANSWER BODY (everything before `## Bibliography` /
17	    `## Evidence-support disclosure`) still exceeds the scorer's truncation budget — so a
18	    silently-truncated answer is caught, never shipped as a complete-looking report,
19	  - asserts idx <-> task-id alignment (scoring report-X against task-Y's rubrics is the
20	    §-1.4 silently-wrong-number class).
21	
22	EVAL TOOLING ONLY. It never edits the scored claims or their citations — it removes only
23	provably-non-answer junk. Faithfulness is untouched. LAW VI: all caps are env-overridable.
24	"""
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import argparse
28	import json
29	import os
30	import re
31	import sys
32	from pathlib import Path
33	
34	MAX_PAPER_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PACK_DRB2_MAX_PAPER_CHARS", "150000"))
35	MAX_LINE_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PACK_DRB2_MAX_LINE_CHARS", "5000"))
36	
37	# I-beatboth-011 idx 15 (#1289): the DRB-II task catalog. A run's internal id (e.g. "task72") does
38	# NOT equal its DRB-II `idx` field (task72 -> idx 56), so packing AI-labor prose with the wrong
39	# `--idx 72` would score it against idx-72's task (Parkinson's) — a silent near-zero (§-1.4
40	# silently-wrong-number). The topic-overlap guard below resolves the task at `--idx` and refuses a
41	# report whose H1/opening shares ZERO content words with that task's (English) description.
42	_DEFAULT_TASKS_JSONL = (
43	    Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "third_party" / "DeepResearch-Bench-II" / "tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl"
44	)
45	# Content word for topic-overlap: a >=3-char alpha token (drops the structural stopwords). Lowercased.
46	_TOPIC_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z][a-z'-]{2,}")
47	_TOPIC_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
48	    "the", "and", "for", "with", "this", "that", "from", "into", "over", "under", "about", "review",
49	    "report", "research", "study", "analysis", "impact", "role", "case", "based", "using", "between",
50	    "their", "these", "those", "which", "while", "have", "has", "are", "was", "were", "its", "via",
51	})
52	
53	# A single-`#` H1 (`^#\s` — `## `/`### ` never match) is treated as a FOREIGN source
54	# masthead ONLY when it appears AFTER report body sections (`## `) have begun. POLARIS's own
55	# document title `# Research report` (which appears BEFORE any `## `) is KEPT (Codex over-strip
56	# catch: do not strip the report's own title).
57	_H1_RE = re.compile(r"^#\s+\S")
58	_BASE64_IMG_RE = re.compile(r"data:image/[^;]+;base64,", re.IGNORECASE)
59	# Gap-stub META-TEXT = a pipeline-internal disclosure about a slot/claim that did not verify.
60	# Keyed on PIPELINE-INTERNAL markers that never appear in legitimate answer prose (so a real
61	# sentence containing "was redacted"/"insufficient evidence" is NEVER stripped — Codex
62	# over-strip catch). Covers both observed stub templates: the "previously stated here"
63	# placeholder and the "did not survive {strict,4-role} verification ... curator-actionable gap
64	# ... this slot is a ..." contract-coverage disclosure.
65	_GAP_STUB_RE = re.compile(
66	    r"(?i)a claim previously stated here|"
67	    r"did not survive (strict|4-role|four-role|d8|provenance)[- ]?verification|"
68	    r"did not survive (strict|4-role|four-role) verification|"
69	    r"curator-actionable gap|human_gap_tasks|frame_coverage_report|"
70	    r"placeholder for (a|the) (claim|finding|sentence)|"
71	    r"this slot is a [a-z-]*\s*gap|"
72	    # N4 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): plain-English gap disclosure (carries [N]).
73	    r"insufficient verified evidence"
74	)
75	# The answer-body boundary: the appendix sections that are NOT rubric-scored answer content.
76	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344): ALSO match the T5 audit-machinery appendix header the render emits
77	# (run_honest_sweep_r3._AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY: "## Appendix: audit, disclosure, and
78	# weighting (not scored as report claims)"). It carries a trailing parenthetical, so its branch
79	# allows any trailing content after "weighting" (the anchored `\s*$` on the other three stays).
80	# Without this branch the reliability/audit-counts block (moved to a TYPED trailing appendix so the
81	# scored body opens on a real claim) would fall INSIDE the scored answer, diluting recall / risking
82	# scorer truncation of real body content. Boundary/segmentation only — never drops any report bytes.
83	_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(
84	    r"^##\s+(?:Bibliography|Evidence-support disclosure|References)\s*$"
85	    r"|^##\s+Appendix:\s*audit,\s*disclosure,\s*and\s*weighting\b.*$",
86	    re.IGNORECASE,
87	)
88	
89	
90	def strip_junk(report_md: str) -> tuple[str, dict]:
91	    """Return (cleaned_markdown, stats). Removes ONLY provably-non-answer junk: foreign
92	    source-masthead H1s (a `# ` heading appearing AFTER `## ` body sections — the report's own
93	    `# ` title is kept), oversized swallowed lines that are NOT markdown table rows, base64
94	    image payloads, and the specific gap-stub placeholder template. Deterministic; no LLM."""
95	    out_lines: list[str] = []
96	    stats = {"masthead_h1": 0, "oversized_lines": 0, "base64_lines": 0, "gap_stub_lines": 0}
97	    seen_h2 = False
98	    for line in report_md.splitlines():
99	        stripped = line.lstrip()
100	        if stripped.startswith("## "):
101	            seen_h2 = True
102	        # foreign masthead H1 — only AFTER body sections began (keep the report's own title).
103	        if _H1_RE.match(line) and seen_h2:
104	            stats["masthead_h1"] += 1
105	            continue
106	        # oversized swallowed line — but PRESERVE wide markdown table rows (`|`-led).
107	        if len(line) > MAX_LINE_CHARS and not stripped.startswith("|"):
108	            stats["oversized_lines"] += 1
109	            continue
110	        if _BASE64_IMG_RE.search(line):
111	            stats["base64_lines"] += 1
112	            continue
113	        if _GAP_STUB_RE.search(line):
114	            stats["gap_stub_lines"] += 1
115	            continue
116	        out_lines.append(line)
117	    return "\n".join(out_lines).strip() + "\n", stats
118	
119	
120	def answer_body(cleaned_md: str) -> str:
121	    """The rubric-scored answer = everything BEFORE the first appendix boundary
122	    (## Bibliography / ## Evidence-support disclosure). The scorer truncates the tail, so
123	    the answer must fit the budget for a fair, non-truncated score."""
124	    lines = cleaned_md.splitlines()
125	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
126	        if _APPENDIX_BOUNDARY_RE.match(line):
127	            return "\n".join(lines[:i]).strip() + "\n"
128	    return cleaned_md
129	
130	
131	def _topic_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
132	    """Lowercased content-word set for topic-overlap (drops structural stopwords)."""
133	    return {w for w in _TOPIC_WORD_RE.findall((text or "").lower()) if w not in _TOPIC_STOPWORDS}
134	
135	
136	def _load_drb2_tasks(tasks_jsonl: Path | None) -> list[dict]:
137	    """Load the DRB-II task catalog (or [] when the file is absent/unreadable — the topic guard then
138	    no-ops with a warning rather than blocking a legitimate run whose data file is not present)."""
139	    if not tasks_jsonl or not Path(tasks_jsonl).exists():
140	        return []
141	    out: list[dict] = []
142	    for line in Path(tasks_jsonl).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").splitlines():
143	        line = line.strip()
144	        if not line:
145	            continue
146	        try:
147	            out.append(json.loads(line))
148	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
149	            continue
150	    return out
151	
152	
153	def _resolve_idx_for_task_name(task_name: str, records: list[dict]) -> str | None:
154	    """The DRB-II `idx` field of the record whose `id` == task_name (e.g. "task72" -> "56")."""
155	    for r in records:
156	        if str(r.get("id")) == str(task_name):
157	            return str(r.get("idx"))
158	    return None
159	
160	
161	def _report_topic_text(report_md: str) -> str:
162	    """The report's TOPIC surface: its first `# ` H1 plus the opening ~2000 chars of prose. Robust
163	    against a generic H1 — a correctly-paired report's opening always names its own subject."""
164	    h1 = ""
165	    for line in (report_md or "").splitlines():
166	        if _H1_RE.match(line):
167	            h1 = line.lstrip("#").strip()
168	            break
169	    return f"{h1}\n{(report_md or '')[:2000]}"
170	
171	
172	def _assert_drb2_topic_match(idx: int, report_md: str, records: list[dict]) -> None:
173	    """FAIL LOUD (SystemExit) if the report's topic shares ZERO content words with the DRB-II task at
174	    `idx` (resolved by the record's `idx` FIELD, the id<->idx offset). Uses the task's English
175	    `description` (robust to zh prompts). No-ops when the catalog is unavailable or either side yields
176	    no extractable topic words (never a false positive). Fires exactly on a wrong-idx mispairing
177	    (AI-labor report vs idx-72 Parkinson's = zero shared words); never on a correct run."""
178	    if not records:
179	        print("[pack_drb2] WARN: DRB-II task catalog unavailable; idx<->topic guard skipped.")
180	        return
181	    rec = next((r for r in records if str(r.get("idx")) == str(idx)), None)
182	    if rec is None:
183	        print(f"[pack_drb2] WARN: no DRB-II task with idx={idx} in the catalog; topic guard skipped.")
184	        return
185	    desc_words = _topic_words(str(rec.get("description") or ""))
186	    report_words = _topic_words(_report_topic_text(report_md))
187	    if not desc_words or not report_words:
188	        return  # cannot judge — do not false-positive
189	    if not (desc_words & report_words):
190	        raise SystemExit(
191	            f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD topic mismatch at --idx {idx}: the report shares ZERO content "
192	            f"words with DRB-II idx {idx} (id={rec.get('id')!r}, {str(rec.get('description'))[:90]!r}). "
193	            f"You are almost certainly packing against the WRONG idx — the id<->idx offset is real "
194	            f"(id 'task72' is idx 56, NOT 72). Pass the correct --idx (or --task-name). "
195	            f"(§-1.4 silently-wrong-number)"
196	        )
197	
198	
199	def pack_one(
200	    report_md_path: Path,
201	    idx: int,
202	    *,
203	    task_id: object | None = None,
204	    task_name: str | None = None,
205	    tasks_jsonl: Path | None = None,
206	    out_dir: Path,
207	    model: str = "polaris",
208	    strict_truncation: bool = True,
209	) -> Path:
210	    """Pack ONE report.md into out_dir/<model>/idx-<idx>.md. Returns the written path.
211	
212	    `task_id` (the DRB-II tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl idx) must equal `idx` — a mismatch means
213	    we would score this report against the WRONG task's rubrics (FAIL LOUD).
214	
215	    I-beatboth-011 idx 15 (#1289): two stronger guards close the silently-wrong-idx landmine the
216	    `task_id == idx` check misses (it passes when BOTH are wrong-but-equal):
217	      (A) `task_name` (e.g. "task72") is resolved to its DRB-II `idx` field via the catalog and
218	          asserted == `idx`, so the real id<->idx offset (task72 -> idx 56) is enforced in code.
219	      (B) a topic-overlap guard refuses a report whose opening shares zero content words with the
220	          task at `idx` (the AI-labor-vs-Parkinson's mispairing)."""
221	    if task_id is not None and int(task_id) != int(idx):
222	        raise SystemExit(
223	            f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD idx<->task mismatch: idx={idx} but task_id={task_id}. "
224	            f"Scoring against the wrong rubrics is forbidden (§-1.4 silently-wrong-number)."
225	        )
226	    records = _load_drb2_tasks(tasks_jsonl)
227	    # (A) id<->idx resolution: a `--task-name` must map (by the catalog's `idx` field) to `--idx`.
228	    if task_name is not None:
229	        resolved = _resolve_idx_for_task_name(task_name, records)
230	        if resolved is None and records:
231	            raise SystemExit(
232	                f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD: --task-name {task_name!r} not found in the DRB-II catalog."
233	            )
234	        if resolved is not None and str(resolved) != str(idx):
235	            raise SystemExit(
236	                f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD id<->idx offset: --task-name {task_name!r} resolves to DRB-II "
237	                f"idx {resolved}, but --idx {idx} was given. The run id ('task72') is NOT the idx "
238	                f"(56) — pass --idx {resolved}. (§-1.4 silently-wrong-number)"
239	            )
240	    report_md = report_md_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
241	    # (B) topic-overlap belt-and-suspenders (fires on a wrong-idx mispairing, never on a correct run).
242	    _assert_drb2_topic_match(idx, report_md, records)
243	    cleaned, stats = strip_junk(report_md)
244	    body = answer_body(cleaned)
245	    if strict_truncation and len(body) > MAX_PAPER_CHARS:
246	        raise SystemExit(
247	            f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD: answer body {len(body)} chars > scorer budget "
248	            f"{MAX_PAPER_CHARS} even after junk-strip {stats} — the scorer would silently "
249	            f"truncate scored content (the run-#7 bug). Fix the generator/render (shorten "
250	            f"the answer or move appendices) before submitting; set strict_truncation=False "
251	            f"only to MEASURE the truncation, never to hide it."
252	        )
253	    out = out_dir / model / f"idx-{idx}.md"
254	    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
255	    out.write_text(cleaned, encoding="utf-8")
256	    return out
257	
258	
259	def main() -> None:
260	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Pack a POLARIS report.md for DeepResearch-Bench-II.")
261	    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True, help="path to POLARIS report.md")
262	    ap.add_argument("--idx", required=True, type=int, help="DRB-II task idx (the catalog `idx` field, NOT the run id number)")
263	    ap.add_argument("--task-id", type=int, default=None, help="tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl idx (asserted == --idx)")
264	    ap.add_argument("--task-name", default=None, help="DRB-II task id e.g. 'task72' (resolved to its idx via the catalog and asserted == --idx)")
265	    ap.add_argument("--tasks-jsonl", default=str(_DEFAULT_TASKS_JSONL), help="DRB-II tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl (for the id<->idx + topic-overlap guards)")
266	    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", default="report", help="output root (report/<model>/idx-N.md)")
267	    ap.add_argument("--model", default="polaris")
268	    ap.add_argument("--allow-truncation", action="store_true", help="measure-only: do NOT fail on over-budget body")
269	    args = ap.parse_args()
270	    out = pack_one(
271	        Path(args.report), args.idx, task_id=args.task_id,
272	        task_name=args.task_name, tasks_jsonl=Path(args.tasks_jsonl) if args.tasks_jsonl else None,
273	        out_dir=Path(args.out_dir), model=args.model,
274	        strict_truncation=not args.allow_truncation,
275	    )
276	    print(f"[pack_drb2] wrote {out} ({out.stat().st_size} bytes)")
277	
278	
279	if __name__ == "__main__":
280	    main()
281

content.text: Both clean, "strip_junk" is a legitimate descriptive function name for a real behavior (stripping non-answer junk), not the vague "junk" as a name for something undefined. It's borderline but the function does precisely what it says with clear scope — I'll keep it as KEEP-NOTED given the strict bar instruction favors RENAME when in doubt. Let me flag it.

content.content:
1	"""Path-B run gate: fatal preflight + post-run enforcement for the DR head-to-head.
2	
3	I-safety-002b (#925), Codex-APPROVE'd plan v5 (5 review rounds). The existing
4	`model_pin.json` is non-gating telemetry, `OPENROUTER_ALLOW_FALLBACKS` defaults true, and
5	"full power" silently degrades if a retrieval key is missing. This gate makes the run
6	full-power + correctly-modeled + drift-free + secret-safe, ENFORCED not asserted-in-prose.
7	
8	Pure-logic core (this module) is fixture-tested with NO live system. The runner wiring
9	(prompt-capture at the LLM call boundary) consumes these functions.
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import hashlib
15	import hmac
16	import json
17	import os
18	import re
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	from pathlib import Path
21	
22	# Env-var name patterns the runner+client read (Codex: enumerate, do not handpick).
23	_CONTROL_PREFIXES = ("PG_", "OPENROUTER_")
24	# A var name is a SECRET (redact value) iff it looks like a credential. NOTE: exclude the
25	# many *_MAX_TOKENS / *_TOKEN_BUDGET knobs — those are config, not secrets.
26	_SECRET_SUFFIXES = ("_API_KEY", "_SECRET", "_ACCESS_TOKEN", "_AUTH_TOKEN")
27	_SECRET_EXPLICIT = {
28	    "OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "SERPER_API_KEY", "SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY",
29	    "EXA_API_KEY", "OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY",
30	    # Codex PR-2 diff iter-1 P2: PG_PATHB_GATE_SALT is the HMAC salt used by
31	    # build_effective_config to redact secrets — it MUST not be persisted in the pin.
32	    "PG_PATHB_GATE_SALT",
33	}
34	_REQUIRED_RETRIEVAL_CREDS = ("SERPER_API_KEY", "SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY")
35	# Run-affecting env that is NOT PG_*/OPENROUTER_* prefixed — must still be in the config hash
36	# (Codex P1: retrieval creds/knobs were missing from the whole-surface enumeration).
37	_EXTRA_CONTROL_ENV = (
38	    "SERPER_API_KEY", "SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY", "EXA_API_KEY", "OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY",
39	)
40	
41	
42	def full_control_surface(roots: list[Path]) -> list[str]:
43	    """Complete run-affecting env set = enumerated PG_*/OPENROUTER_* UNION the extra
44	    retrieval/credential env (Codex P1 — these affect the run but aren't prefix-matched)."""
45	    return sorted(set(enumerate_control_surface(roots)) | set(_EXTRA_CONTROL_ENV))
46	
47	
48	def control_surface_sources(roots: list[Path]) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
49	    """name -> sorted ['<relpath>:<lineno>', ...] provenance for each control var (Codex P2)."""
50	    extra = "|".join(re.escape(n) for n in _EXTRA_CONTROL_ENV)
51	    pat = re.compile(
52	        rf"os\.(?:getenv|environ\.get)\(\s*['\"]((?:PG_|OPENROUTER_)[A-Z0-9_]+|{extra})['\"]"
53	        rf"|os\.environ\[\s*['\"]((?:PG_|OPENROUTER_)[A-Z0-9_]+|{extra})['\"]"
54	    )
55	    out: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
56	    for root in roots:
57	        for py in root.rglob("*.py"):
58	            try:
59	                lines = py.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines()
60	            except OSError:
61	                continue
62	            for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
63	                for m in pat.finditer(line):
64	                    name = m.group(1) or m.group(2)
65	                    out.setdefault(name, set()).add(f"{py.as_posix()}:{i}")
66	    return {k: sorted(v) for k, v in out.items()}
67	
68	# I-meta-002 PR-9/M4: a lock `serving_route` starting with this prefix is a self-hosted vLLM
69	# verifier box (Mirror / Sentinel / Judge), NOT OpenRouter. Self-host roles skip OpenRouter
70	# resolution at preflight and are checked via the served {endpoint, model} (no provider_name).
71	_SELF_HOST_ROUTE_PREFIX = "vast_self_host"
72	# Per-role self-host endpoint env-var stem (mirrors openai_compatible_transport, LAW VI).
73	_SELF_HOST_BASE_URL_ENV_TEMPLATE = "PG_{role}_BASE_URL"
74	
75	# Volatile response fields EXCLUDED from the served-identity surrogate (Codex iter-5 P2).
76	_VOLATILE_METADATA_FIELDS = frozenset(
77	    {"id", "request_id", "created", "timestamp", "usage", "prompt_tokens",
78	     "completion_tokens", "total_tokens", "latency", "latency_ms", "cost", "x-request-id"}
79	)
80	
81	
82	class GateError(RuntimeError):
83	    """Fatal gate violation — the run is INVALID and must be discarded, never scored."""
84	
85	
86	def is_secret_var(name: str) -> bool:
87	    """True iff `name` is a credential whose VALUE must be redacted (not a *_TOKENS knob)."""
88	    if name in _SECRET_EXPLICIT:
89	        return True
90	    if name.endswith(("_MAX_TOKENS", "_TOKENS", "_TOKEN_BUDGET")):
91	        return False
92	    return name.endswith(_SECRET_SUFFIXES)
93	
94	
95	def enumerate_control_surface(roots: list[Path]) -> list[str]:
96	    """Scan source for every PG_*/OPENROUTER_* env var actually read (Codex: complete set).
97	
98	    Greps os.getenv / os.environ.get / os.environ[...] reads — NOT a handpicked list.
99	    """
100	    pat = re.compile(
101	        r"os\.(?:getenv|environ\.get)\(\s*['\"]((?:PG_|OPENROUTER_)[A-Z0-9_]+)['\"]"
102	        r"|os\.environ\[\s*['\"]((?:PG_|OPENROUTER_)[A-Z0-9_]+)['\"]"
103	    )
104	    found: set[str] = set()
105	    for root in roots:
106	        for py in root.rglob("*.py"):
107	            try:
108	                text = py.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
109	            except OSError:
110	                continue
111	            for m in pat.finditer(text):
112	                found.add(m.group(1) or m.group(2))
113	    return sorted(found)
114	
115	
116	def _redact(name: str, value: str | None, salt: bytes) -> dict:
117	    """Secret presence record: present/length/salted-HMAC only — never the value (Codex P1)."""
118	    if value is None:
119	        return {"present": False, "length": 0, "salted_hmac": None}
120	    digest = hmac.new(salt, value.encode("utf-8"), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
121	    return {"present": True, "length": len(value), "salted_hmac": digest}
122	
123	
124	def build_effective_config(control_vars: list[str], salt: bytes) -> dict:
125	    """Snapshot the full control surface; secret VALUES redacted (Codex P1)."""
126	    cfg: dict[str, dict] = {}
127	    for name in control_vars:
128	        val = os.environ.get(name)
129	        if is_secret_var(name):
130	            cfg[name] = {"secret": True, **_redact(name, val, salt)}
131	        else:
132	            cfg[name] = {"secret": False, "value": val, "set": val is not None}
133	    return cfg
134	
135	
136	def effective_config_hash(cfg: dict) -> str:
137	    return hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(cfg, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
138	
139	
140	def served_identity(call_metadata: dict) -> str:
141	    """Stable per-role served-identity surrogate (Codex iter-4 P1 + iter-5 P2).
142	
143	    OpenRouter has no stable `model_version`; use provider_name + model + system_fingerprint
144	    (+ any stable router metadata), EXCLUDING volatile fields. Missing the agreed surrogate
145	    fields is caught by the caller (fatal), not silently hashed to a constant here.
146	    """
147	    stable = {
148	        k: v for k, v in call_metadata.items()
149	        if k not in _VOLATILE_METADATA_FIELDS
150	    }
151	    return hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(stable, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
152	
153	
154	@dataclass
155	class RolePin:
156	    role: str            # "generator" | "evaluator" | "mirror" | "sentinel" | "judge"
157	    model_slug: str      # FULL OpenRouter slug, e.g. "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro" — EXACT match
158	    provider_name: str
159	    surrogate_fields: tuple[str, ...]   # the metadata fields PROVEN present at preflight
160	    canonical_slug: str | None = None   # I-bug-945 (#931): OpenRouter dated snapshot resolved
161	    # at preflight via GET /api/v1/models. The alias `model_slug` is the env-pin handle; the
162	    # canonical_slug (e.g. deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro-20260423) is what gets served as `model`
163	    # in chat completions and is the actual pre-registration anchor in pathB_gate_pin.json.
164	    # Trailing defaulted field per Codex P2#3 (don't break positional RolePin call sites).
165	    serving_route: str | None = None    # I-meta-002 PR-9/M4: the lock-sourced serving_route
166	    # for this role (e.g. "openrouter" | "vast_self_host" | "vast_self_host_bf16"). When it
167	    # starts with "vast_self_host", preflight takes the self-host branch (NO OpenRouter
168	    # resolution) and assert_post_run enforces served==pinned via _pathb_served instead of
169	    # provider_name. None / "openrouter" => the unchanged OpenRouter path. Trailing defaulted.
170	    base_url: str | None = None          # I-meta-002 PR-9/M4: the configured PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL
171	    # captured at preflight (trailing slash stripped). assert_post_run compares the served
172	    # endpoint to THIS pinned value (drift-safe — PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL is built via .format() so
173	    # it is not in the grepped control surface / config-drift hash). Trailing defaulted.
174	    allow_provider_drift: bool = False   # I-judge-kimi (2026-06-29): True ONLY for a deliberately
175	    # load-balanced OpenRouter role (the unpinned kimi Judge, spread across its 21 endpoints so the
176	    # per-claim D8 burst never 429-tears the seam). When True, assert_post_run RELAXES the
177	    # single-provider equality + the provider surrogate-drift check FOR THIS ROLE — the provider is
178	    # allowed to vary across calls. It does NOT relax the served==pinned MODEL check or the OpenRouter
179	    # route requirement (both NEVER relaxed). Default False => every pinned/self-host role keeps the
180	    # strict single-provider gate byte-for-byte. Set by pathB_runner._role_pins on the benchmark Judge.
181	
182	
183	def _role_surrogate(metadata: dict, surrogate_fields: tuple[str, ...] | list) -> str:
184	    """The served-identity surrogate over the PINNED fields only (Codex P1 — exact pin)."""
185	    picked = {f: metadata.get(f) for f in surrogate_fields}
186	    return hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(picked, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
187	
188	
189	def preflight(
190	    control_vars: list[str],
191	    role_pins: list[RolePin],
192	    salt: bytes,
193	    reachability_prober=None,
194	    source_map: dict[str, list[str]] | None = None,
195	    roots: list[Path] | None = None,
196	    offline: bool = False,
197	    enforce_architecture_coverage: bool = True,
198	) -> dict:
199	    """Fatal preflight. Returns the pin record to hash-pin BEFORE the run.
200	
201	    The gate FORCES the complete control surface (Codex iter-2 P1): it always unions the
202	    retrieval/credential env into `control_vars`, and if `roots` is given it also unions the
203	    grepped PG_*/OPENROUTER_* surface — so a caller passing `[]` cannot produce an empty config.
204	
205	    Reachability is enforce-by-default for real runs (Codex iter-2 P1): if `offline` is False
206	    and no prober is given, `real_retrieval_prober` is used (live ping). Tests pass
207	    `offline=True` to skip the network. `reachability_checked=False` is therefore only
208	    possible in an explicitly-offline (test) run, never a real run gate.
209	    """
210	    # FORCE the complete control surface (retrieval creds always; grepped surface if roots given)
211	    surface = set(control_vars or [])
212	    if roots:
213	        surface |= set(full_control_surface(roots))
214	    surface |= set(_EXTRA_CONTROL_ENV)
215	    control_vars = sorted(surface)
216	    # reachability: enforce-by-default for real runs
217	    if not offline and reachability_prober is None:
218	        reachability_prober = real_retrieval_prober
219	    # 1. fallbacks OFF
220	    if os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_ALLOW_FALLBACKS", "true").strip().lower() not in ("false", "0", "no"):
221	        raise GateError("OPENROUTER_ALLOW_FALLBACKS must be false (it defaults TRUE)")
222	    # 2. provider routing — per-role resolution at preflight (I-bug-946 #932).
223	    # The env supplies a candidate list (comma-separated). Each role's actual served provider
224	    # is resolved via OpenRouter's per-model endpoints endpoint and pinned per role. The old
225	    # "singleton" check rejected multi-provider orders, but disjoint model provider sets
226	    # require a multi-entry order (e.g. fireworks for deepseek-v4-pro, novita for gemma).
227	    order = (os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER") or "").strip()
228	    provider_order_list = [p.strip() for p in order.split(",") if p.strip()]
229	    if not provider_order_list:
230	        raise GateError("OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER must list at least one candidate provider")
231	    # 3. required retrieval capability — PRESENCE *and* REACHABILITY (Codex P1)
232	    missing = [c for c in _REQUIRED_RETRIEVAL_CREDS if not os.environ.get(c)]
233	    if missing:
234	        raise GateError(f"required retrieval credentials absent — not full-power: {missing}")
235	    if reachability_prober is not None:
236	        for cred, backend in _REQUIRED_RETRIEVAL_CREDS_TO_BACKENDS.items():
237	            if not reachability_prober(backend):
238	                raise GateError(f"retrieval backend {backend!r} unreachable/invalid-key — not full-power")
239	    # I-meta-002 PR-9/M4: lock-sourced role -> serving_route map. Self-host roles
240	    # (serving_route: vast_self_host*) DO NOT resolve via OpenRouter; the generator
241	    # (serving_route: openrouter) and any role absent from the lock keep the OpenRouter path.
242	    serving_routes = _role_serving_routes()
243	    # 4. each role pin must declare which surrogate fields it PROVED present + a full slug
244	    for rp in role_pins:
245	        if not rp.surrogate_fields:
246	            raise GateError(f"role {rp.role}: no served-identity surrogate fields proven present")
247	        if "/" not in rp.model_slug:
248	            raise GateError(f"role {rp.role}: model_slug must be the FULL slug (provider/model), got {rp.model_slug!r}")
249	        # I-meta-002 PR-9/M4 self-host branch: a self-hosted vLLM verifier (Mirror / Sentinel /
250	        # Judge) is NOT on OpenRouter. Validate its PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL is configured (fail-closed,
251	        # LAW VI: a self-host role with no endpoint is a deployment error, never a silent default)
252	        # and record the pinned base_url + serving_route for the post-run served==pinned check.
253	        # NO network here (env presence + lock read only — the live /v1/models identity probe is
254	        # the M2 canary, not preflight). Skip canonical_slug + OpenRouter provider resolution.
255	        # I-judge-kimi (2026-06-29): RESPECT a serving_route the caller PRE-SET on the pin. The
256	        # benchmark-aware Judge pin (pathB_runner._role_pins) sets serving_route='openrouter' to
257	        # override the lock's sovereign self-host route for the kimi Judge served over OpenRouter.
258	        # Only an UNSET (None) route is sourced from the lock — every existing caller passes
259	        # serving_route=None, so this is byte-identical for them (lock-sourced) and only the
260	        # benchmark Judge's explicit 'openrouter' override is preserved.
261	        if rp.serving_route is None:
262	            rp.serving_route = serving_routes.get(rp.role)
263	        route = rp.serving_route
264	        if _is_self_host_route(route):
265	            base_url_env = _SELF_HOST_BASE_URL_ENV_TEMPLATE.format(role=rp.role.upper())
266	            base_url = os.environ.get(base_url_env)
267	            if not base_url:
268	                raise GateError(
269	                    f"role {rp.role}: self-host serving_route {route!r} requires {base_url_env} "
270	                    f"to be set (the self-hosted endpoint must be configured, LAW VI)"
271	                )
272	            rp.base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
273	            continue
274	        # I-bug-945 (#931): resolve OpenRouter alias to its dated canonical_slug at preflight.
275	        # The catalog is the single source of truth; fail closed if the alias is unknown.
276	        # Skipped on offline runs (unit tests pass canonical_slug directly when needed).
277	        if not offline and rp.canonical_slug is None:
278	            rp.canonical_slug = resolve_canonical_slug(rp.model_slug)
279	        # I-bug-946 (#932): resolve per-role provider via /api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints.
280	        # Codex iter-1 diff P1: online preflight MUST always overwrite provider_name from
281	        # the resolver, because _role_pins() previously pre-seeded provider_name from the
282	        # env's first entry (now changed to empty, but the always-overwrite invariant is
283	        # the defense-in-depth — any future caller passing a non-empty provider_name still
284	        # gets re-resolved at preflight, so the persisted pin reflects the actual resolution).
285	        # Offline tests skip the resolver; if provider_name is empty under offline, fall
286	        # back to the env's first entry (test-compat — tests still drive the pipeline via
287	        # OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER and expect the served provider to match the pin).
288	        if not offline:
289	            rp.provider_name = resolve_role_provider(rp.model_slug, provider_order_list)
290	        elif not rp.provider_name and provider_order_list:
291	            rp.provider_name = provider_order_list[0]
292	    # I-bug-946 (Codex iter 2 P2#3 + iter-1 diff P2#2): enforce that the effective
293	    # entailment model equals the effective evaluator model. Compare EFFECTIVE values
294	    # (with their defaults) so a single env override that diverges from the default still
295	    # fails closed — not only the "both env vars set divergently" case.
296	    _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.1"  # I-arch-003 #1253: sovereign mirror (was stale gemma)
297	    _DEFAULT_EVALUATOR_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.1"   # I-arch-003 #1253: sovereign mirror (was stale gemma)
298	    eff_entail = (os.environ.get("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL") or _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL).strip()
299	    eff_eval = (os.environ.get("PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL") or _DEFAULT_EVALUATOR_MODEL).strip()
300	    if eff_entail != eff_eval:
301	        raise GateError(
302	            f"effective PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL={eff_entail!r} != effective PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL"
303	            f"={eff_eval!r}; the gate cannot pin two different evaluator-family models"
304	        )
305	    # I-meta-001 (#933) Step 9: enforce architecture coverage at preflight.
306	    # If config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml exists, its required_roles set
307	    # MUST match the role_pins set (every locked role pinned). Gate refuses if missing.
308	    # offline=True implies test mode (unit fixtures intentionally use 2-role pins);
309	    # tests can also explicitly opt out via enforce_architecture_coverage=False.
310	    _enforce_coverage = enforce_architecture_coverage and not offline
311	    arch_coverage = (
312	        _assert_architecture_coverage(role_pins) if _enforce_coverage
313	        else {"status": "skipped", "missing_roles": [], "lock_status": None,
314	              "warning": "architecture coverage check skipped (offline=True or enforce=False)"}
315	    )
316	    cfg = build_effective_config(control_vars, salt)
317	    return {
318	        "effective_config": cfg,
319	        "effective_config_hash": effective_config_hash(cfg),
320	        "control_vars": control_vars,             # the RESOLVED surface (post-run rebuilds from this)
321	        "control_source_map": source_map or {},   # name -> [file:line, ...] provenance (Codex P2)
322	        "role_pins": [vars(rp) | {"surrogate_fields": list(rp.surrogate_fields)} for rp in role_pins],
323	        "architecture_coverage": arch_coverage,  # I-meta-001 Step 9
324	        "reachability_checked": reachability_prober is not None,
325	        "openrouter_allow_fallbacks": False,
326	        "openrouter_provider_order": order,
327	    }

329	
330	def _assert_architecture_coverage(role_pins: list) -> dict:
331	    """I-meta-001 (#933) Step 9: refuse the smoke unless every locked architecture role
332	    is present in role_pins.
333	
334	    Loads ``config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml``. If the lock exists AND
335	    its status is ``locked``, every ``required_roles`` key must appear in role_pins.
336	    If status is ``codex_approved_pending_operator_signature`` (the explicit operator
337	    freeze state), the gate refuses ALL smokes — smokes resume only after the lock is
338	    promoted to ``locked`` via scripts/architecture/verify_lock.py.
339	
340	    When the lock file is missing (legacy path / pre-Step-4 commits), behavior is
341	    permissive: accept any role_pins shape but emit a warning in the returned dict.
342	
343	    Returns a dict {"status": ..., "missing_roles": [...], "lock_status": ...}.
344	    Raises GateError when coverage is enforced and violated.
345	    """
346	    from pathlib import Path
347	    lock_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "config" / "architecture" / "polaris_runtime_lock.yaml"
348	    if not lock_path.exists():
349	        return {"status": "no_lock", "missing_roles": [], "lock_status": None,
350	                "warning": "no config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml — coverage not enforced"}
351	
352	    try:
353	        import yaml  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
354	        lock = yaml.safe_load(lock_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
355	    except Exception as exc:
356	        raise GateError(f"architecture lock unreadable: {exc}") from exc
357	
358	    lock_status = lock.get("status")
359	    required_roles = set(lock.get("required_roles", {}).keys())
360	    pinned_roles = {rp.role for rp in role_pins}
361	    missing = required_roles - pinned_roles
362	
363	    if lock_status == "codex_approved_pending_operator_signature":
364	        raise GateError(
365	            "architecture lock status is 'codex_approved_pending_operator_signature' — "
366	            "smokes FROZEN per operator directive (I-meta-001 #933). Promote the lock to "
367	            "status: locked via scripts/architecture/verify_lock.py after all propagation "
368	            "checkpoints are complete + tests pass + operator commits."
369	        )
370	
371	    if lock_status == "locked" and missing:
372	        raise GateError(
373	            f"architecture coverage incomplete: locked roles {required_roles!r} "
374	            f"but pinned only {pinned_roles!r}; missing {missing!r}. The 4-role "
375	            f"architecture requires generator + mirror + sentinel + judge."
376	        )
377	
378	    return {
379	        "status": "ok" if not missing else "partial",
380	        "missing_roles": sorted(missing),
381	        "lock_status": lock_status,
382	        "required_roles": sorted(required_roles),
383	        "pinned_roles": sorted(pinned_roles),
384	    }
385	
386	
387	def _role_serving_routes() -> dict[str, str]:
388	    """I-meta-002 PR-9/M4: map ``role -> serving_route`` from the runtime architecture lock.
389	
390	    Reads ``config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml`` (the single machine-readable source
391	    of truth). The serving_route tells preflight + assert_post_run which roles are self-hosted
392	    vLLM boxes (``serving_route: vast_self_host*``) vs OpenRouter (``serving_route: openrouter``).
393	
394	    Degrades gracefully (Codex M4 design): a missing / unreadable lock returns ``{}`` so EVERY
395	    role falls through to the unchanged OpenRouter path. This keeps the offline
396	    generator+evaluator unit fixtures green — ``evaluator`` is not in the lock (=> not self-host)
397	    and ``generator`` is ``serving_route: openrouter`` (=> OpenRouter path unchanged). This helper
398	    is independent of the freeze in ``_assert_architecture_coverage``: it NEVER raises on lock
399	    status (the spend-freeze stays solely in that function, M4 criterion #3).
400	    """
401	    lock_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "config" / "architecture" / "polaris_runtime_lock.yaml"
402	    if not lock_path.exists():
403	        return {}
404	    try:
405	        import yaml  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
406	        lock = yaml.safe_load(lock_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
407	    except Exception:
408	        return {}
409	    required = (lock or {}).get("required_roles") or {}
410	    routes: dict[str, str] = {}
411	    for role, spec in required.items():
412	        route = (spec or {}).get("serving_route")
413	        if route:
414	            routes[role] = str(route)
415	    return routes
416	
417	
418	def _is_self_host_route(serving_route: str | None) -> bool:
419	    """True iff ``serving_route`` designates a self-hosted vLLM box (vast_self_host*)."""
420	    return bool(serving_route) and serving_route.startswith(_SELF_HOST_ROUTE_PREFIX)
421	
422	
423	def _self_host_endpoint_surrogate(served_model: str, served_endpoint: str) -> str:
424	    """Single-valued served-identity surrogate for a self-host role.
425	
426	    A self-hosted vLLM response carries NO provider_name / system_fingerprint, so the served
427	    identity is the (model, endpoint) pair (Codex M4). Used for the per-role no-mid-run-drift
428	    check in assert_post_run — distinct from the OpenRouter surrogate over surrogate_fields.
429	    """
430	    picked = {"model": served_model, "endpoint": served_endpoint}
431	    return hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(picked, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
432	
433	
434	def resolve_role_provider(model_slug: str, provider_order: list[str]) -> str:
435	    """I-bug-946 (#932): resolve a role's served provider at preflight.
436	
437	    OpenRouter's GET /api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints returns the providers that actually serve
438	    a model. Smoke #15 demonstrated that without per-role resolution, OpenRouter silently
439	    routes a role to a provider OUTSIDE the env-pinned order (gemma is not on Fireworks, so
440	    the evaluator was routed to Novita despite `OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER=fireworks` +
441	    `allow_fallbacks=false`). The gate caught it correctly; the pin model was wrong.
442	
443	    Algorithm:
444	      1. Fetch /api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints; parse `data.endpoints`.
445	      2. Eligible = endpoints with status absent OR status==0. status != 0 means degraded /
446	         lower-priority per OpenRouter docs (Codex iter-2 P2#4).
447	      3. Intersect eligible providers (case-insensitive) with `provider_order`.
448	      4. Return the catalog-cased provider_name of the FIRST match in `provider_order`.
449	      5. Fail closed with diagnostic if no match OR endpoints list is empty.
450

451	    Codex APPROVE iter 2 on choice C, brief at .codex/I-bug-946/brief_iter2.md.
452	    """
453	    import requests
454	    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
455	    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}"} if api_key else {}
456	    url = f"https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models/{model_slug}/endpoints"
457	    try:
458	        r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=20)
459	        r.raise_for_status()
460	        endpoints = r.json().get("data", {}).get("endpoints", [])
461	    except Exception as exc:
462	        raise GateError(f"OpenRouter endpoints catalog unreachable for {model_slug!r}: {exc}") from exc
463	    if not endpoints:
464	        raise GateError(f"OpenRouter has no endpoints for model {model_slug!r} (model offline?)")
465	    eligible: list[str] = []
466	    for ep in endpoints:
467	        status = ep.get("status")
468	        if status is None or status == 0:
469	            name = ep.get("provider_name")
470	            if name:
471	                eligible.append(name)
472	    if not eligible:
473	        raise GateError(
474	            f"OpenRouter has no eligible (status==0) endpoints for {model_slug!r}; "
475	            f"all endpoints degraded"
476	        )
477	    eligible_lower = {n.lower(): n for n in eligible}  # case-insensitive lookup, catalog cased value
478	    for wanted in provider_order:
479	        catalog_cased = eligible_lower.get(wanted.strip().lower())
480	        if catalog_cased is not None:
481	            return catalog_cased
482	    raise GateError(
483	        f"OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER={provider_order!r} has no intersection with eligible "
484	        f"endpoints for {model_slug!r}; available: {sorted(set(eligible))}"
485	    )

488	def resolve_canonical_slug(model_slug: str) -> str | None:
489	    """I-bug-945 (#931): Resolve OpenRouter alias to its dated canonical_slug at preflight.
490	
491	    OpenRouter exposes a list at GET /api/v1/models with `id` (alias) + `canonical_slug`
492	    (dated snapshot) per entry. The chat-completions response returns `model=<canonical_slug>`
493	    while the env pin uses `<id>`. Resolving at preflight lets `assert_post_run` accept the
494	    served canonical_slug without losing pre-registration integrity — the resolved value is
495	    persisted in `pathB_gate_pin.json` and becomes the audit anchor.
496	
497	    Returns the canonical_slug, or None if it equals model_slug (no dated suffix exposed).
498	    Raises GateError if the slug is not in the catalog (fail closed) — Codex P2#2.
499	    """
500	    import requests
501	    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
502	    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}"} if api_key else {}
503	    try:
504	        r = requests.get("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models", headers=headers, timeout=20)
505	        r.raise_for_status()
506	        data = r.json().get("data", [])
507	    except Exception as exc:
508	        raise GateError(f"OpenRouter models catalog unreachable: {exc}") from exc
509	    for m in data:
510	        if m.get("id") == model_slug:
511	            cs = m.get("canonical_slug")
512	            return cs if cs and cs != model_slug else None
513	    raise GateError(f"OpenRouter catalog has no entry for pinned slug {model_slug!r} (alias unknown)")
514

516	def real_retrieval_prober(backend: str) -> bool:
517	    """Minimal live reachability ping per backend (Codex P1). Returns True iff a valid
518	    response. Cheap; runs once at preflight. Imported lazily to keep the module light."""
519	    import requests
520	    try:
521	        if backend == "serper":
522	            r = requests.post(
523	                "https://google.serper.dev/search",
524	                headers={"X-API-KEY": os.environ["SERPER_API_KEY"], "Content-Type": "application/json"},
525	                json={"q": "metformin"}, timeout=15,
526	            )
527	            return r.status_code == 200
528	        if backend == "semantic_scholar":
529	            r = requests.get(
530	                "https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search",
531	                headers={"x-api-key": os.environ["SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY"]},
532	                params={"query": "metformin", "limit": 1}, timeout=20,
533	            )
534	            return r.status_code == 200
535	    except Exception:
536	        return False
537	    return False

540	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
541	# I-ready-017 CANARY-01 (#1108): behavioral pre-spend canary.
542	# The drb_72 held run's preflight checked CONFIG + the 4 VERIFIER slugs but NOT the
543	# searcher/generator generate_structured call shape (the FX-01-keystone 404 class that silently
544	# killed agentic discovery) nor a real 1-query search. A dead route was SWALLOWED -> a "green" run on
545	# dead discovery (~7 sources from "1000 URLs"). This canary tests BEHAVIOR — real call shapes, every
546	# probed component ALIVE — and FAILS CLOSED before any full-run spend. chromium is intentionally NOT
547	# probed: the benchmark fetch path is httpx (live_retriever), not a headless browser; chromium
548	# readiness is FX-16 (VM-side).
549	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
550	_BEHAVIORAL_CANARY_ENV = "PG_BEHAVIORAL_CANARY"
551	
552	
553	async def _default_structured_output_probe() -> bool:
554	    """REAL generate_structured call on the default generator/searcher slug (OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL —
555	    the deepseek reasoning-first model whose structured-output 404 was the FX-01 keystone). Returns
556	    True iff a schema object parses; raises GateError on the NoEndpointError/404 class. Tiny schema +
557	    budget keep it cheap; the 404 failure path returns immediately (before any generation).
558	
559	    ASYNC (Codex iter-1 P1): awaited directly by the async behavioral_canary() — NOT wrapped in
560	    asyncio.run(), which raises RuntimeError inside run_gate_b_query's already-running event loop and
561	    would block every healthy live run."""
562	    from pydantic import BaseModel
563	
564	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
565	        PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
566	        NoEndpointError,
567	        OpenRouterClient,
568	    )
569	
570	    class _CanaryProbe(BaseModel):
571	        ok: bool
572	
573	    # Codex iter-2 P1: probe the EXACT effective generator/searcher slug the live STORM/agentic
574	    # structured calls use (PG_GENERATOR_MODEL > OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL precedence — the same
575	    # constant multi_section_generator/the sweep import), NOT bare OpenRouterClient() (which would
576	    # check OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL and could pass while the real generator slug's structured-output
577	    # path is dead — recreating the green-on-dead-discovery failure).
578	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=PG_GENERATOR_MODEL)
579	    try:
580	        result = await client.generate_structured(
581	            prompt='Reply with JSON only: {"ok": true}.',
582	            schema=_CanaryProbe,
583	            max_tokens=128,
584	        )
585	        return result is not None
586	    except NoEndpointError as exc:
587	        raise GateError(
588	            "behavioral canary: structured-output probe got NoEndpointError on the searcher/generator "
589	            f"slug — the FX-01-keystone 404 class that silently kills discovery. Aborting BEFORE spend. "
590	            f"({exc})"
591	        )
592	    finally:
593	        await client.close()

596	def _default_live_search_probe() -> int:
597	    """REAL primary-backend search call shape (the function the pipeline actually uses); returns the
598	    live result count. >0 confirms discovery produces sources (the drb_72 collapse returned ~7)."""
599	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import _serper_search
600	
601	    return len(_serper_search("metformin efficacy in type 2 diabetes", num=3))

604	async def behavioral_canary(
605	    *,
606	    structured_output_probe=_default_structured_output_probe,
607	    live_search_probe=_default_live_search_probe,
608	) -> None:
609	    """Pre-spend BEHAVIORAL canary (CANARY-01, #1108). FAIL CLOSED (GateError) unless the REAL call
610	    shapes the full run depends on are ALIVE — NOT a config-flag check. Prints BEHAVIORAL_CANARY_OK on
611	    success. Gated by PG_BEHAVIORAL_CANARY (slate-activated for the run); a no-op when off.
612	
613	    ASYNC (Codex iter-1 P1): awaited from the async run_gate_b_query() BEFORE spend. The
614	    structured-output probe is a coroutine (awaited); the live-search probe is sync (a one-shot
615	    requests call). ALL probe failures normalize to GateError (Codex iter-1 P2: the fail-closed
616	    contract — an arbitrary exception must not leak as a non-GateError abort).
617	
618	    Probes (the gap the drb_72 'green run on dead discovery' preflight missed):
619	      1. structured-output on the searcher/generator slug — the FX-01-keystone generate_structured 404
620	         class (the old preflight checked only the 4 VERIFIER slugs).
621	      2. a real 1-query primary-backend search returning >0 live results — catches the silent
622	         discovery-collapse.
623	
624	    The probes are injectable so the canary LOGIC is unit-tested offline (faked dead/alive) without
625	    network; the LIVE invocation (real probes) runs pre-spend on the real Gate-B run.
626	    """
627	    if os.getenv(_BEHAVIORAL_CANARY_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in ("1", "true"):
628	        return  # opt-in; off = no-op (byte-unchanged behavior)
629	    try:
630	        structured_ok = await structured_output_probe()
631	    except GateError:
632	        raise
633	    except Exception as exc:  # fail-closed contract: any probe failure -> GateError
634	        raise GateError(
635	            f"behavioral canary: structured-output probe failed ({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}) — "
636	            f"fail closed BEFORE spend."
637	        )
638	    if not structured_ok:
639	        raise GateError(
640	            "behavioral canary: structured-output probe returned no parsed object on the "
641	            "searcher/generator slug — the structured-output path is degraded. Aborting BEFORE spend."
642	        )
643	    try:
644	        n_sources = live_search_probe()
645	    except GateError:
646	        raise
647	    except Exception as exc:
648	        raise GateError(
649	            f"behavioral canary: live-search probe failed ({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}) — fail closed "
650	            f"BEFORE spend."
651	        )
652	    if n_sources <= 0:
653	        raise GateError(
654	            "behavioral canary: 1-query primary-backend search returned 0 live sources — discovery is "
655	            "degraded (the drb_72 silent-collapse: ~7 sources from '1000 URLs'). Aborting BEFORE spend."
656	        )
657	    print("BEHAVIORAL_CANARY_OK", flush=True)

660	@dataclass
661	class LLMCall:
662	    call_id: str
663	    role: str
664	    prompt_messages_present: bool
665	    request_hash: str | None
666	    response_metadata: dict   # served {model, provider_name, system_fingerprint, ...}
667	
668	
669	def assert_post_run(
670	    pin: dict,
671	    control_vars: list[str],
672	    salt: bytes,
673	    calls: list[LLMCall],
674	    retrieval_backends_attempted: set[str],
675	) -> dict:
676	    """Fatal post-run gate (Codex). Any violation ⇒ run INVALID, discard + re-run.
677	
678	    Returns the established per-role served-identity surrogates on success."""
679	    # config no-drift — rebuild from the SAME resolved surface preflight pinned (not a
680	    # caller-passed list, which could differ / bypass; Codex iter-2).
681	    pinned_vars = pin.get("control_vars", control_vars)
682	    cfg_now = effective_config_hash(build_effective_config(pinned_vars, salt))
683	    if cfg_now != pin["effective_config_hash"]:
684	        raise GateError("effective_config drifted between preflight and post-run")
685	    pins_by_role = {rp["role"]: rp for rp in pin["role_pins"]}
686	    if not calls:
687	        raise GateError("no LLM calls captured — completeness check cannot pass")
688	    # COMPLETENESS: every pinned role must appear (Codex P1 — uncaptured evaluator/judge invisible)
689	    roles_seen = {c.role for c in calls}
690	    missing_roles = set(pins_by_role) - roles_seen
691	    if missing_roles:
692	        raise GateError(f"completeness: pinned role(s) with no captured LLM call: {missing_roles}")
693	    surrogate_by_role: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
694	    for c in calls:
695	        if not (c.prompt_messages_present and c.request_hash and c.response_metadata):
696	            raise GateError(f"call {c.call_id}: incomplete capture (prompt/request_hash/metadata)")
697	        rp = pins_by_role.get(c.role)
698	        if rp is None:
699	            raise GateError(f"call {c.call_id}: role {c.role!r} not pinned")
700	        # I-meta-002 PR-9/M4 self-host branch: a self-hosted vLLM verifier (Mirror / Sentinel /
701	        # Judge) carries NO provider_name — its served identity is the M1 `_pathb_served`
702	        # {endpoint, model}, which pathB_capture.build_response_metadata flattens onto the
703	        # captured metadata as top-level `model` + `endpoint` keys (provider_name/
704	        # system_fingerprint are dropped for a vLLM response). Read those flattened keys and
705	        # fail-closed assert served model == pinned model_slug AND served endpoint == the
706	        # PINNED base_url (drift-safe: PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL is not in the config-drift hash;
707	        # comparing against the value pinned at preflight is what catches a wrong-box serve).
708	        # Missing endpoint and/or model => the `_pathb_served` block never reached capture =>
709	        # fatal. This branch fires BEFORE the surrogate-field / provider OpenRouter checks
710	        # (which would spuriously fail on a self-host call that has no provider_name).
711	        if _is_self_host_route(rp.get("serving_route")):
712	            served_model = c.response_metadata.get("model")
713	            served_endpoint = c.response_metadata.get("endpoint")
714	            if served_model is None or served_endpoint is None:
715	                raise GateError(
716	                    f"call {c.call_id}: self-host role {c.role!r} captured no served identity "
717	                    f"(_pathb_served endpoint/model missing): model={served_model!r} "
718	                    f"endpoint={served_endpoint!r}"
719	                )
720	            pinned_model = rp["model_slug"]
721	            if served_model != pinned_model:
722	                raise GateError(
723	                    f"call {c.call_id}: self-host role {c.role!r} served model {served_model!r} "
724	                    f"!= pinned model_slug {pinned_model!r}"
725	                )
726	            pinned_base_url = (rp.get("base_url") or "")
727	            if pinned_base_url.rstrip("/") != served_endpoint.rstrip("/"):
728	                raise GateError(
729	                    f"call {c.call_id}: self-host role {c.role!r} served endpoint "
730	                    f"{served_endpoint!r} != pinned base_url {rp.get('base_url')!r}"
731	                )
732	            surrogate_by_role.setdefault(c.role, set()).add(
733	                _self_host_endpoint_surrogate(served_model, served_endpoint.rstrip("/"))
734	            )
735	            continue
736	        for fld in rp["surrogate_fields"]:
737	            if fld not in c.response_metadata:
738	                raise GateError(f"call {c.call_id}: served metadata missing surrogate field {fld!r}")
739	        # I-judge-kimi (2026-06-29): a role marked allow_provider_drift is served by a DELIBERATELY
740	        # load-balanced OpenRouter route (the unpinned kimi Judge spread across its 21 endpoints to
741	        # dodge the per-claim 429 seam-tear) — so it serves MANY providers in one run BY DESIGN. For
742	        # such a role the single-provider equality + the provider surrogate-drift check are RELAXED.
743	        # What is NEVER relaxed: (a) the served MODEL must still be the pinned slug (alias/canonical),
744	        # and (b) the ROUTE must be OpenRouter — which reaching this branch already proves, since a
745	        # self-host role `continue`d above. The model-identity surrogate below stays single-valued
746	        # (provider_name dropped from the drift set), so a mid-run model swap still fails closed.
747	        allow_provider_drift = bool(rp.get("allow_provider_drift"))
748	        # EXACT provider + EXACT full-slug model match (Codex P1 — no loose substring).
749	        # I-bug-944 (#925 smoke #13): provider comparison is case-insensitive (OpenRouter
750	        # returns "Fireworks" / "DeepInfra" with title case while the pin env var
751	        # OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER is documented lower-case; case-mismatch is identity-
752	        # equivalent and must not gate-fail an otherwise full-power run). Model slug stays
753	        # case-sensitive — slugs are canonical.
754	        served_provider = (c.response_metadata.get("provider_name") or "").strip().lower()
755	        pinned_provider = (rp["provider_name"] or "").strip().lower()
756	        if not allow_provider_drift and served_provider != pinned_provider:
757	            raise GateError(f"call {c.call_id}: served provider {c.response_metadata.get('provider_name')!r} != pinned {rp['provider_name']!r}")
758	        # I-bug-945 (#931): accept served model matching EITHER the pinned alias OR the
759	        # canonical_slug resolved at preflight. The OpenRouter chat-completions response
760	        # returns `model=<canonical_slug>` while the env pin is the alias; both are identity-
761	        # equivalent and recorded in the persisted pin (pathB_gate_pin.json). NEVER relaxed —
762	        # the served==pinned MODEL check holds even for a provider-drift role.
763	        served_model = c.response_metadata.get("model")
764	        accepted_models = {rp["model_slug"]}
765	        if rp.get("canonical_slug"):
766	            accepted_models.add(rp["canonical_slug"])
767	        if served_model not in accepted_models:
768	            raise GateError(
769	                f"call {c.call_id}: served model {served_model!r} matches neither pinned "
770	                f"alias {rp['model_slug']!r} nor canonical_slug {rp.get('canonical_slug')!r}"
771	            )
772	        # I-bug-945 P2#4 (Codex): normalize served `model` to the alias before surrogate
773	        # compute, so a same-role mix of alias and canonical_slug calls produces a single
774	        # surrogate value (otherwise raw-model drift would false-fail the mid-run check).
775	        normalized_metadata = dict(c.response_metadata)
776	        normalized_metadata["model"] = rp["model_slug"]
777	        # I-judge-kimi: for a provider-drift role drop provider_name from the per-role drift
778	        # surrogate so the mid-run drift check enforces ONLY the (single-valued) MODEL identity;
779	        # the provider is allowed to vary. Every other role keeps the full surrogate_fields
780	        # (e.g. ('provider_name','model')) so its single-provider drift gate is byte-identical.
781	        drift_fields = (
782	            tuple(f for f in rp["surrogate_fields"] if f != "provider_name")
783	            if allow_provider_drift
784	            else rp["surrogate_fields"]
785	        )
786	        surrogate_by_role.setdefault(c.role, set()).add(_role_surrogate(normalized_metadata, drift_fields))
787	    # per-role served identity must be SINGLE-VALUED across the run (no mid-run drift, Codex P1)
788	    for role, ids in surrogate_by_role.items():
789	        if len(ids) != 1:
790	            raise GateError(f"role {role}: served-identity surrogate drifted across calls ({len(ids)} distinct)")
791	    # required retrieval backends were ACTUALLY attempted (Codex iter-5 P2)
792	    required = set(_REQUIRED_RETRIEVAL_CREDS_TO_BACKENDS.values())
793	    not_attempted = required - retrieval_backends_attempted
794	    if not_attempted:
795	        raise GateError(f"required retrieval backends never attempted this run: {not_attempted}")
796	    return {"served_identity_by_role": {r: next(iter(ids)) for r, ids in surrogate_by_role.items()}}
797	
798	
799	_REQUIRED_RETRIEVAL_CREDS_TO_BACKENDS = {
800	    "SERPER_API_KEY": "serper",
801	    "SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY": "semantic_scholar",
