1	"""Targeted single-source MAP probe (#1217 Bug B diagnostic).
2	
3	Runs the production ``distill_section_evidence`` on ONE evidence source for ONE
4	section, with ``PG_DISTILL_DEBUG=1`` reject tracing on, and dumps the per-source
5	``CoverageRow`` (status + n_findings + "N proposed, M validated" reason) plus every
6	validated finding's claim/quote. This answers, for the one source that matters
7	(the CDC safety source [4] whose findings legacy mined but the distill ledger
8	dropped): did the MAP propose findings, and if so which validation STEP killed
9	them — without running a full 8-source A/B.
10	
11	Faithfulness: this ORCHESTRATES the real production distiller (zero gate drift).
12	The live MAP call is gated behind ``--live`` (default OFF) so the module imports
13	+ unit-tests offline. One source = ~one LLM call (~1 cent).
14	
15	Usage (live, authorized spend, on the OVH VM):
16	    PG_DISTILL_DEBUG=1 python scripts/dr_benchmark/probe_source_map.py --live \
17	        --pool <evidence_pool.json> \
18	        --evidence-id probiotic_immunocompromised_contraindication \
19	        --section-title "Safety and contraindications" \
20	        --section-focus "Safety, adverse events, and contraindications of the intervention"
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import argparse
26	import asyncio
27	import json
28	import os
29	import sys
30	from pathlib import Path
31	from typing import Any, Optional
32	
33	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
34	if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
35	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
36	
37	
38	def load_pool(path: str | Path) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
39	    raw = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
40	    rows = raw if isinstance(raw, list) else list(raw.values())
41	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
42	    for i, row in enumerate(rows):
43	        eid = row.get("evidence_id")
44	        if not eid:
45	            raise ValueError(f"evidence row {i} has no evidence_id")
46	        pool[eid] = row
47	    return pool
48	
49	
50	async def run_probe(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
51	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.evidence_distiller import distill_section_evidence
52	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan
53	
54	    pool = load_pool(args.pool)
55	    if args.evidence_id not in pool:
56	        raise SystemExit(f"evidence_id {args.evidence_id!r} not in pool ({len(pool)} rows)")
57	    row = pool[args.evidence_id]
58	    dq = row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("text") or ""
59	    print(f"[probe] source={args.evidence_id} dq_len={len(dq)} model={args.model}")
60	
61	    section = SectionPlan(title=args.section_title, focus=args.section_focus,
62	                          ev_ids=[args.evidence_id])
63	    dist = await distill_section_evidence(
64	        section, [row], pool, model=args.model, max_parallel=1,
65	    )
66	
67	    print(f"\n[probe] === COVERAGE ({len(dist.coverage)} row(s)) ===")
68	    for c in dist.coverage:
69	        print(f"  status={getattr(c,'status','?')} n_findings={getattr(c,'n_findings','?')} "
70	              f"reason={getattr(c,'reason','')!r}")
71	    print(f"\n[probe] === VALIDATED FINDINGS ({len(dist.findings)}) ===")
72	    for f in dist.findings:
73	        print(f"  - claim={f.claim!r}")
74	        print(f"    numbers={f.numbers} atom_ids={f.atom_ids} quote={f.support_quote[:120]!r}")
75	    if not dist.findings:
76	        print("  (none — see the [DISTILL_DEBUG] REJECT lines above for the killing step)")
77	    return 0
78	
79	
80	def build_arg_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
81	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Single-source MAP probe (#1217 Bug B).")
82	    p.add_argument("--live", action="store_true",
83	                   help="Make a REAL MAP LLM call (authorized spend). Default OFF.")
84	    p.add_argument("--pool", type=str, required=False,
85	                   default="outputs/audits/I-perm-010/run_drb76_iter2/drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc/evidence_pool.json")
86	    p.add_argument("--evidence-id", type=str, default="probiotic_immunocompromised_contraindication")
87	    p.add_argument("--section-title", type=str, default="Safety and contraindications")
88	    p.add_argument("--section-focus", type=str,
89	                   default="Safety, adverse events, and contraindications of the intervention")
90	    p.add_argument("--model", type=str, default=os.getenv("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"))
91	    return p
92	
93	
94	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
95	    args = build_arg_parser().parse_args(argv)
96	    if not args.live:
97	        print("probe_source_map: refusing to run the LIVE MAP call without --live "
98	              "(it makes a real billable LLM call). Re-run with --live on the OVH VM.")
99	        return 0
100	    return asyncio.run(run_probe(args))
101	
102	
103	if __name__ == "__main__":
104	    raise SystemExit(main())
105

1	"""Query-generation COVERAGE-ISOLATION harness (I-qgen-001, GH #1291).
2	
3	Operator method (2026-06-22): test ONLY query generation, fast, no e2e. A query-gen
4	method's only job is COVERAGE — getting the right evidence into the corpus. So we measure
5	exactly that and nothing downstream:
6	
7	    run the method's queries -> retrieve -> for each REQUIRED point (a DRB-II info_recall
8	    rubric) ask "is its evidence present in the retrieved corpus?" -> coverage fraction.
9	
10	NO report generation, NO rendering, NO DeepTRACE judge here — those gate the faithfulness
11	sections + the final combined run. ISOLATION: every method sees the SAME retrieve() and the
12	SAME coverage judge(); only the queries differ. The canonical question is bound via
13	gate0_lineage so coverage is scored against the RIGHT rubrics (the drb_72 wrong-question fix).
14	
15	retrieve() and judge() are injected (the runner wires POLARIS retrieval + the GLM-5.2 judge),
16	so this module is deterministic and unit-testable on stubs.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import json
22	import os
23	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
24	from typing import Any, Callable, Protocol, runtime_checkable
25	
26	from scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage import (
27	    DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH,
28	    GateZeroLineageError,
29	    SLUG_TO_IDX,
30	    load_canonical_question,
31	)
32	
33	# query -> list of evidence rows [{"url": str, "text": str}]. In a real run this is wrapped
34	# in a deterministic per-query snapshot cache so every method sees identical retrieval.
35	RetrieveFn = Callable[[str], list[dict[str, str]]]
36	# (required_point, corpus_text) -> True iff the corpus carries the evidence for the point.
37	CoverageJudgeFn = Callable[[str, str], bool]
38	
39	
40	def load_required_points(idx: int, tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH) -> list[str]:
41	    """The info_recall rubric points (the required evidence) for a canonical DRB-II idx.
42	
43	    These are exactly what a complete corpus must let the report cover. Fail loud if the
44	    idx or its rubric is missing — never a silent empty list.
45	    """
46	    if not os.path.isfile(tasks_path):
47	        raise GateZeroLineageError(f"required-points: tasks file not found: {tasks_path}")
48	    with open(tasks_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
49	        for line in handle:
50	            line = line.strip()
51	            if not line:
52	                continue
53	            record = json.loads(line)
54	            if record.get("idx") == idx:
55	                rubric = (record.get("content") or {}).get("rubric") or {}
56	                points = rubric.get("info_recall")
57	                if not points:
58	                    raise GateZeroLineageError(
59	                        f"required-points: idx={idx} has no info_recall rubric"
60	                    )
61	                return [str(p) for p in points]
62	    raise GateZeroLineageError(f"required-points: idx={idx} not found in {tasks_path}")
63	
64	
65	def load_blocked_references(idx: int, tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH) -> dict | None:
66	    """The DRB-II BLOCKED reference for an idx (the source the report must NOT view/cite), or
67	    None if the task has none.
68	
69	    DRB-II's blocked mechanism is what forces INDEPENDENT multi-source synthesis: a method that
70	    "covers" a point only via the forbidden source has cheated, and DRB-II scores that support as
71	    INVALID. So the harness must exclude the blocked source before scoring coverage. Shape in the
72	    gold file: ``{"title": str, "authors": [str], "urls": [str]}``. Raises only if the idx itself
73	    is absent (a bad idx); an absent blocked FIELD is legitimate -> None.
74	    """
75	    if not os.path.isfile(tasks_path):
76	        raise GateZeroLineageError(f"blocked-refs: tasks file not found: {tasks_path}")
77	    with open(tasks_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
78	        for line in handle:
79	            line = line.strip()
80	            if not line:
81	                continue
82	            record = json.loads(line)
83	            if record.get("idx") == idx:
84	                # Ground truth (verified idx 1-56): the blocked reference lives at content.blocked
85	                # (top-level 'blocked' is absent). We read content.blocked PRIMARY and fall back to
86	                # a top-level 'blocked' so the loader is correct under either layout — robust to any
87	                # row-format drift (Codex iter-3 flagged a top-level path; this makes the question moot).
88	                blocked = (record.get("content") or {}).get("blocked")
89	                if not (isinstance(blocked, dict) and blocked):
90	                    blocked = record.get("blocked")
91	                return blocked if isinstance(blocked, dict) and blocked else None
92	    raise GateZeroLineageError(f"blocked-refs: idx={idx} not found in {tasks_path}")
93	
94	
95	def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
96	    """Normalize a URL for blocked-source matching: drop scheme, leading www, trailing slash,
97	    query/fragment. Lossy on purpose so mirror URLs of the same paper still match."""
98	    u = (url or "").strip().lower()
99	    for prefix in ("https://", "http://"):
100	        if u.startswith(prefix):
101	            u = u[len(prefix) :]
102	    if u.startswith("www."):
103	        u = u[4:]
104	    u = u.split("#", 1)[0].split("?", 1)[0]
105	    return u.rstrip("/")
106	
107	
108	def make_blocked_filter(blocked: dict | None) -> Callable[[dict], bool]:
109	    """Return ``is_blocked(row) -> bool``: True iff a retrieved row is the forbidden source.
110	
111	    Matches on URL (normalized, against every blocked mirror URL) OR on the blocked title
112	    appearing in the row text. No-op (always False) when there is no blocked reference.
113	    """
114	    if not blocked:
115	        return lambda row: False
116	    blocked_urls = {_normalize_url(u) for u in (blocked.get("urls") or []) if u}
117	    blocked_title = " ".join((blocked.get("title") or "").split()).lower()
118	
119	    def is_blocked(row: dict) -> bool:
120	        url = _normalize_url(row.get("url") or "")
121	        if url and url in blocked_urls:
122	            return True
123	        text = " ".join((row.get("text") or "").split()).lower()
124	        if blocked_title and len(blocked_title) >= 20 and blocked_title in text:
125	            return True
126	        return False
127	
128	    return is_blocked
129	
130	
131	@dataclass
132	class CoverageBudget:
133	    """Identical budget every method runs under (isolation: only query quality varies)."""
134	
135	    max_query_rounds: int = field(
136	        default_factory=lambda: int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_MAX_QUERY_ROUNDS", "4"))
137	    )
138	    max_queries: int = field(
139	        default_factory=lambda: int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_MAX_QUERIES", "24"))
140	    )
141	
142	
143	@runtime_checkable
144	class QueryGenMethod(Protocol):
145	    """A query-generation method under test. Its ONLY job: assemble the best corpus.
146	
147	    `name` identifies it; `generate_corpus` runs its (possibly iterative/closed-loop) query
148	    logic against the SHARED retrieve(), returning the assembled corpus. Methods may NOT
149	    summarize/transform evidence text (common code owns that downstream); they only choose
150	    WHAT to search and WHICH retrieved rows to keep.
151	    """
152	
153	    name: str
154	
155	    def generate_corpus(
156	        self, question: str, retrieve: RetrieveFn, budget: CoverageBudget
157	    ) -> list[dict[str, str]]: ...
158	
159	
160	@dataclass
161	class CoverageResult:
162	    method: str
163	    idx: int
164	    covered: int
165	    total: int
166	    coverage: float
167	    n_sources: int
168	    n_queries_issued: int
169	    per_point: list[dict[str, Any]]
170	    # Blocked (forbidden) rows this method retrieved and that were DROPPED before scoring — so a
171	    # method cannot win coverage via the prohibited DRB-II source. >0 means the method DID surface
172	    # the blocked source (telemetry; the rows were excluded from the corpus regardless).
173	    blocked_dropped: int = 0
174	
175	
176	def score_coverage(
177	    method_name: str,
178	    idx: int,
179	    required_points: list[str],
180	    corpus: list[dict[str, str]],
181	    judge: CoverageJudgeFn,
182	    n_queries_issued: int,
183	) -> CoverageResult:
184	    """Coverage = fraction of required points whose evidence is present in the corpus."""
185	    corpus_text = "\n\n".join((row.get("text") or "") for row in corpus)
186	    per_point: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
187	    covered = 0
188	    for point in required_points:
189	        is_covered = bool(judge(point, corpus_text))
190	        # Keep the FULL rubric point (not truncated) so the §-1.1 audit can read exactly which
191	        # required point was/was not covered; a short preview rides alongside for skim-reading.
192	        per_point.append(
193	            {"point": point, "point_preview": point[:160], "covered": is_covered}
194	        )
195	        if is_covered:
196	            covered += 1
197	    total = len(required_points)
198	    return CoverageResult(
199	        method=method_name,
200	        idx=idx,
201	        covered=covered,
202	        total=total,
203	        coverage=(covered / total) if total else 0.0,
204	        n_sources=len(corpus),
205	        n_queries_issued=n_queries_issued,
206	        per_point=per_point,
207	    )
208	
209	
210	def run_coverage_test(
211	    idx: int,
212	    methods: list[QueryGenMethod],
213	    retrieve: RetrieveFn,
214	    judge: CoverageJudgeFn,
215	    budget: CoverageBudget | None = None,
216	    tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH,
217	    blocked_refs: dict | None = None,
218	) -> list[CoverageResult]:
219	    """Run every method on the canonical question, score coverage, return ranked results.
220	
221	    The canonical question + required points come straight from the gold file (gate0_lineage),
222	    so the wrong-question failure cannot recur. Every method gets the SAME retrieve()/judge().
223	
224	    blocked_refs (the DRB-II forbidden source, via load_blocked_references): when provided, any
225	    retrieved row matching it is DROPPED before it can reach a method's corpus — uniformly for
226	    every method — so coverage cannot be won by retrieving the prohibited source (Codex iter-2 P1).
227	    """
228	    budget = budget or CoverageBudget()
229	    question = load_canonical_question(idx, tasks_path)
230	    required_points = load_required_points(idx, tasks_path)
231	    is_blocked = make_blocked_filter(blocked_refs)
232	
233	    results: list[CoverageResult] = []
234	    for method in methods:
235	        counting_retrieve, counter = _counting(retrieve)
236	        blocked_count = {"n": 0}
237	
238	        def blocked_filtered(query: str, _cr=counting_retrieve, _bc=blocked_count):
239	            kept: list[dict[str, str]] = []
240	            for row in _cr(query):
241	                if is_blocked(row):
242	                    _bc["n"] += 1  # forbidden source: count it, never let it into the corpus
243	                else:
244	                    kept.append(row)
245	            return kept
246	
247	        corpus = method.generate_corpus(question, blocked_filtered, budget)
248	        result = score_coverage(
249	            method.name, idx, required_points, corpus, judge, counter["n"]
250	        )
251	        result.blocked_dropped = blocked_count["n"]
252	        results.append(result)
253	    results.sort(key=lambda r: r.coverage, reverse=True)
254	    return results
255	
256	
257	def _counting(retrieve: RetrieveFn) -> tuple[RetrieveFn, dict[str, int]]:
258	    """Wrap retrieve() to count how many queries a method issued (isolation telemetry)."""
259	    counter = {"n": 0}
260	
261	    def wrapped(query: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
262	        counter["n"] += 1
263	        return retrieve(query)
264	
265	    return wrapped, counter
266

1	"""Faithful frontier query-gen adapters for the I-qgen-001 bake-off (GH #1291).
2	
3	Each adapter implements ONE named method's PORTABLE query-generation scaffold, built from its
4	primary arXiv source (research roster: outputs/qgen_coverage/frontier_roster.json). Every adapter
5	exposes generate_corpus(question, retrieve, budget) and uses the SHARED retrieve() + an injected
6	GLM-5.2 `llm` — so only the query LOGIC differs, never the retriever or model (the bake-off
7	isolates query-gen). budget.max_queries caps retrieve() calls equally for every method.
8	
9	These are the method-SPECIFIC scaffolds, not a generic gap loop:
10	  WebWeaver           - outline-centric ReAct: search/write-outline/terminate; re-query off the evolving outline
11	  IterResearch        - report-centric workspace RECONSTRUCTION (O(1) memory; report overwritten each round)
12	  ConvergeWriter      - fixed two-stage breadth(seed kw)->depth(per-doc grounded kw); bottom-up, no gap loop
13	  WARP (AgentCPM)     - writing-as-reasoning: per-section query conditioned on the DRAFT; lazy Expand on thin sections
14	  DuMate              - DAG roadmap + ready-frontier + ephemeral RUBRIC as gap-list/stop predicate
15	  FS-Researcher       - index.md TOC + todo queue + fixed 6-item checklist re-query
16	  ScaffoldAgent       - mutable outline TREE + UCB node selection + Expansion/Revision/Contraction ops
17	  PokeeResearch       - research<->verification scaffold (3 failure modes); multi-query per call
18	  DOLORES             - recursive meta-decomposition (associate/compute/recurse); query emerges from ASSOCIATE
19	
20	DeepResearch-R1/9K (2603.01152) is trained-only (query logic in the weights) -> NOT adapted here
21	(noted, not faked, per the standard-process rule).
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import math
27	import re
28	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
29	from typing import Callable
30	
31	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_coverage_harness import CoverageBudget, RetrieveFn
32	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_methods import _dedup_keep
33	
34	LlmFn = Callable[[str], str]
35	
36	
37	def _lines(text: str, cap: int = 12) -> list[str]:
38	    """Parse an LLM reply into clean query/line items (strip numbering/bullets)."""
39	    out: list[str] = []
40	    for raw in (text or "").splitlines():
41	        s = raw.strip()
42	        if not s:
43	            continue
44	        s = re.sub(r"^\s*(?:[-*]|\d+[.):])\s*", "", s).strip().strip('"').strip()
45	        if s and len(s) > 2 and not s.lower().startswith(("here", "sure", "the following")):
46	            out.append(s)
47	        if len(out) >= cap:
48	            break
49	    return out
50	
51	
52	def _digest(rows: list[dict], n: int = 8, chars: int = 160) -> str:
53	    """A short digest of retrieved rows to feed back into the planner context (steers next queries)."""
54	    parts = []
55	    for r in (rows or [])[:n]:
56	        t = " ".join((r.get("text") or "").split())[:chars]
57	        if t:
58	            parts.append(t)
59	    return " | ".join(parts)
60	
61	
62	class _Budgeted:
63	    """Mixin: shared corpus state + a query budget counter for every adapter."""
64	
65	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):  # pragma: no cover - overridden
66	        raise NotImplementedError
67	
68	    def generate_corpus(self, question: str, retrieve: RetrieveFn, budget: CoverageBudget) -> list[dict]:
69	        self._seen: set[str] = set()
70	        self._corpus: list[dict] = []
71	        self._n_queries = 0
72	        self._max = budget.max_queries
73	        self._rounds = budget.max_query_rounds
74	        self._run(question, retrieve, budget)
75	        return self._corpus
76	
77	    def _search(self, retrieve: RetrieveFn, query: str) -> list[dict]:
78	        if self._n_queries >= self._max:
79	            return []
80	        self._n_queries += 1
81	        rows = _dedup_keep(retrieve(query), self._seen)
82	        self._corpus += rows
83	        return rows
84	
85	    @property
86	    def _budget_left(self) -> bool:
87	        return self._n_queries < self._max
88	
89	
90	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- WebWeaver
91	@dataclass
92	class WebWeaverMethod(_Budgeted):
93	    """WebWeaver (2509.13312): outline co-evolves with search; re-query targets under-supported
94	    outline sections; only distilled summaries (not raw pages) steer the next query batch."""
95	
96	    llm: LlmFn
97	    name: str = "webweaver"
98	
99	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
100	        outline = self.llm(
101	            "Draft a concise hierarchical OUTLINE (section titles only, one per line) for a complete "
102	            f"report answering this research question:\n{question}"
103	        )
104	        summaries: list[str] = []
105	        for _ in range(self._rounds * 4):
106	            if not self._budget_left:
107	                break
108	            ctx = "\n".join(summaries[-12:])
109	            q = self.llm(
110	                "You are WebWeaver's planner. Given the question, the current report OUTLINE, and "
111	                "summaries of evidence found so far, list the next search queries that fill the "
112	                "OUTLINE's still-UNSUPPORTED or MISSING sections. One query per line, no prose.\n\n"
113	                f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nOUTLINE:\n{outline}\n\nEVIDENCE SUMMARIES SO FAR:\n{ctx}"
114	            )
115	            qs = _lines(q, cap=6)
116	            if not qs:
117	                break
118	            for query in qs:
119	                rows = self._search(retrieve, query)
120	                if rows:
121	                    summaries.append(_digest(rows))
122	                if not self._budget_left:
123	                    break
124	            # write-outline: restructure the outline from the new evidence (outline-centric re-query)
125	            outline = self.llm(
126	                "Rewrite/restructure this report outline to reflect the new evidence (add, reorder, "
127	                "or split sections). Section titles only, one per line.\n\n"
128	                f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nCURRENT OUTLINE:\n{outline}\n\nNEW EVIDENCE:\n"
129	                + "\n".join(summaries[-8:])
130	            ) or outline
131	
132	
133	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- IterResearch
134	@dataclass
135	class IterResearchMethod(_Budgeted):
136	    """IterResearch/Tongyi (2510.24701 / 2511.07327): report-centric round loop with workspace
137	    RECONSTRUCTION — the prompt each round carries ONLY (question, current report, last obs); the
138	    report is OVERWRITTEN (strategic forgetting, O(1) memory); next query derived from (question, report)."""
139	
140	    llm: LlmFn
141	    name: str = "iterresearch"
142	
143	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
144	        report = ""
145	        last_obs = ""
146	        for _ in range(self._max):
147	            if not self._budget_left:
148	                break
149	            out = self.llm(
150	                "You are IterResearch. Your ONLY memory is the evolving REPORT below (history is "
151	                "discarded). Reason over (question, report, last observation): what is established, "
152	                "what is still missing. Then output exactly two blocks:\n"
153	                "REPORT: <a rewritten, compressed report retaining validated findings + what you just learned>\n"
154	                "QUERY: <ONE next search query, or the word STOP if information is sufficient>\n\n"
155	                f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nREPORT:\n{report or '(empty)'}\n\nLAST OBSERVATION:\n{last_obs or '(none)'}"
156	            )
157	            new_report = ""
158	            query = ""
159	            m = re.search(r"REPORT:\s*(.*?)\s*QUERY:", out, re.S | re.I)
160	            if m:
161	                new_report = m.group(1).strip()
162	            mq = re.search(r"QUERY:\s*(.*)", out, re.S | re.I)
163	            if mq:
164	                query = mq.group(1).strip().splitlines()[0].strip() if mq.group(1).strip() else ""
165	            report = new_report or report  # OVERWRITE: strategic forgetting
166	            if not query or query.upper().startswith("STOP"):
167	                break
168	            rows = self._search(retrieve, query)
169	            last_obs = _digest(rows)
170	
171	
172	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- ConvergeWriter
173	@dataclass
174	class ConvergeWriterMethod(_Budgeted):
175	    """ConvergeWriter (2509.12811): bottom-up, NOT a gap loop. Stage 1 breadth = seed keywords from
176	    the question title; Stage 2 depth = per-retrieved-document grounded keyword expansion. Fixed two stages."""
177	
178	    llm: LlmFn
179	    name: str = "convergewriter"
180	
181	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
182	        # Stage 1 (breadth): seed keyword queries from the question alone.
183	        seeds = _lines(
184	            self.llm(
185	                "Generate breadth-first SEARCH KEYWORDS/queries to begin researching this topic. "
186	                "One per line, no prose.\n\n" + question
187	            ),
188	            cap=8,
189	        )
190	        breadth_cap = max(1, int(self._max * 0.4))
191	        for q in seeds[:breadth_cap]:
192	            self._search(retrieve, q)
193	            if self._n_queries >= breadth_cap:
194	                break
195	        # Stage 2 (depth): per-document grounded keyword expansion over what stage 1 retrieved.
196	        d1 = list(self._corpus)
197	        for row in d1:
198	            if not self._budget_left:
199	                break
200	            doc = " ".join((row.get("text") or "").split())[:1200]
201	            if not doc:
202	                continue
203	            deeper = _lines(
204	                self.llm(
205	                    "Read this retrieved document and generate DEEPER search queries it implies for "
206	                    f"the topic. One per line, no prose.\n\nTOPIC:\n{question}\n\nDOCUMENT:\n{doc}"
207	                ),
208	                cap=3,
209	            )
210	            for q in deeper:
211	                self._search(retrieve, q)
212	                if not self._budget_left:
213	                    break
214	
215	
216	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- WARP / AgentCPM-Report
217	@dataclass
218	class WarpMethod(_Budgeted):
219	    """WARP / AgentCPM-Report (2602.06540): writing-as-reasoning. Sparse Level-1 outline; per-section
220	    query conditioned on (question, section intent, DRAFT so far); thin sections Expand into
221	    sub-sections (lazy hierarchical growth). The DRAFT itself is the gap signal."""
222	
223	    llm: LlmFn
224	    name: str = "warp"
225	
226	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
227	        sections = _lines(
228	            self.llm(
229	                "Emit a SPARSE level-1 outline (high-level section titles only, one per line) for a "
230	                f"report answering:\n{question}"
231	            ),
232	            cap=8,
233	        ) or [question]
234	        draft = ""
235	        deepenings = 0
236	        i = 0
237	        while i < len(sections) and self._budget_left:
238	            section = sections[i]
239	            i += 1
240	            query = self.llm(
241	                "Write ONE search query for this section, conditioned on the question, the section "
242	                "intent, AND the draft so far (so the query EXTENDS the existing argument). Query only.\n\n"
243	                f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nSECTION:\n{section}\n\nDRAFT SO FAR:\n{draft[-1500:] or '(empty)'}"
244	            ).strip().splitlines()
245	            query = query[0].strip() if query else section
246	            rows = self._search(retrieve, query)
247	            draft += f"\n## {section}\n{_digest(rows, n=4)}\n"
248	            # reasoning-driven deepening: diagnose the thinnest section, Expand it (<=12 deepenings, lazy)
249	            if deepenings < 12 and self._budget_left:
250	                exp = self.llm(
251	                    "Re-read the draft. Name the ONE section still too THIN, then list 1-3 sub-section "
252	                    "titles that deepen it (one per line). If the draft is complete, reply DONE.\n\n"
253	                    f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nDRAFT:\n{draft[-2000:]}"
254	                )
255	                if "DONE" not in exp.upper():
256	                    subs = _lines(exp, cap=3)
257	                    if subs:
258	                        sections[i:i] = subs  # insert sub-sections to draft next (hierarchical)
259	                        deepenings += 1
260	
261	
262	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- DuMate
263	@dataclass
264	class DuMateMethod(_Budgeted):
265	    """DuMate (2606.07299): DAG roadmap + ready-frontier (coarse->fine) + an EPHEMERAL RUBRIC
266	    regenerated from new evidence each cycle that is BOTH the next-query driver AND the stop
267	    predicate (no outstanding gap => halt). Inner search does multi-formulation expansion."""
268	
269	    llm: LlmFn
270	    name: str = "dumate"
271	
272	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
273	        outline = self.llm(f"Draft a report outline (section titles, one per line) for:\n{question}")
274	        frontier = _lines(
275	            self.llm(
276	                "Enumerate the coarse research SUB-TASKS needed to cover this outline. One per line.\n\n"
277	                f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nOUTLINE:\n{outline}"
278	            ),
279	            cap=8,
280	        ) or [question]
281	        for _ in range(self._rounds):
282	            if not self._budget_left or not frontier:
283	                break
284	            evidence_digest = ""
285	            for subtask in list(frontier):
286	                if not self._budget_left:
287	                    break
288	                # inner search agent: multi-formulation query expansion for this sub-task
289	                forms = _lines(
290	                    self.llm(
291	                        "Generate 2-3 search query reformulations (broad -> specific) for this "
292	                        "sub-task. One per line, no prose.\n\nSUB-TASK:\n" + subtask
293	                    ),
294	                    cap=3,
295	                ) or [subtask]
296	                for q in forms:
297	                    rows = self._search(retrieve, q)
298	                    evidence_digest += " " + _digest(rows, n=3)
299	                    if not self._budget_left:
300	                        break
301	            # ephemeral rubric = residual-gap list regenerated from new evidence; empty => stop
302	            gaps = _lines(
303	                self.llm(
304	                    "Given the question, outline, and evidence gathered, list the REMAINING research "
305	                    "gaps as new sub-tasks. One per line. If coverage is complete, reply NONE.\n\n"
306	                    f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nOUTLINE:\n{outline}\n\nEVIDENCE:\n{evidence_digest[:3000]}"
307	                ),
308	                cap=6,
309	            )
310	            if not gaps or any("NONE" in g.upper() for g in gaps[:1]):
311	                break
312	            frontier = gaps  # re-plan: expand the frontier with finer gap sub-tasks
313	
314	
315	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- FS-Researcher
316	@dataclass
317	class FsResearcherMethod(_Budgeted):
318	    """FS-Researcher (2602.01566): index.md TOC (topic deconstruction + KB hierarchy) + todo queue;
319	    per-todo LLM query; re-query driven by a FIXED 6-item self-review checklist (esp. 'a question the
320	    KB cannot fully answer' / 'an aspect with only 1-2 weak sources')."""
321	
322	    llm: LlmFn
323	    name: str = "fs_researcher"
324	
325	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
326	        todos = _lines(
327	            self.llm(
328	                "Deconstruct this research topic into sub-topics (the index.md table of contents). "
329	                "One sub-topic per line.\n\n" + question
330	            ),
331	            cap=10,
332	        ) or [question]
333	        notes: list[str] = []
334	        for _ in range(self._rounds):
335	            if not self._budget_left or not todos:
336	                break
337	            for todo in list(todos):
338	                if not self._budget_left:
339	                    break
340	                q = self.llm("Write ONE search query for this sub-topic. Query only.\n\n" + todo).strip()
341	                q = q.splitlines()[0].strip() if q else todo
342	                rows = self._search(retrieve, q)
343	                notes.append(f"[{todo[:50]}] {_digest(rows, n=3)}")
344	            # 6-item Appendix-B checklist critic -> deficient sub-topics become new todos
345	            deficient = _lines(
346	                self.llm(
347	                    "Self-review the knowledge base against: exhaustive coverage (a question the KB "
348	                    "cannot fully answer?) and information density (any aspect with only 1-2 weak "
349	                    "sources?). List sub-topics still needing more search. One per line, or NONE.\n\n"
350	                    f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nNOTES:\n" + "\n".join(notes[-20:])
351	                ),
352	                cap=6,
353	            )
354	            if not deficient or any("NONE" in d.upper() for d in deficient[:1]):
355	                break
356	            todos = deficient
357	
358	
359	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- ScaffoldAgent
360	@dataclass
361	class ScaffoldAgentMethod(_Budgeted):
362	    """ScaffoldAgent (2606.20122): mutable outline TREE; UCB selects the next node (exploit
363	    low-utility + explore under-visited); typed ops Expansion (decompose -> query per child),
364	    Revision (re-query node in place), Contraction (merge siblings, retrieves nothing)."""
365	
366	    llm: LlmFn
367	    name: str = "scaffoldagent"
368	    c_ucb: float = 1.4
369	
370	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
371	        titles = _lines(
372	            self.llm(
373	                "Build a minimal report outline: 3-5 top-level section titles for this question. "
374	                "One per line.\n\n" + question
375	            ),
376	            cap=5,
377	        ) or [question]
378	        # node = {title, context, n, util}
379	        nodes = [{"title": t, "ctx": question, "n": 0, "util": 0.0} for t in titles]
380	        total = 0
381	        for _ in range(max(self._rounds * 4, 20)):
382	            if not self._budget_left or not nodes:
383	                break
384	            total += 1
385	            # UCB select: argmax(-mean_util + c*sqrt(ln N / n)); unvisited first
386	            def ucb(nd):
387	                if nd["n"] == 0:
388	                    return float("inf")
389	                return -nd["util"] + self.c_ucb * math.sqrt(math.log(total + 1) / nd["n"])
390	            node = max(nodes, key=ucb)
391	            op = self.llm(
392	                "Choose ONE operation for this outline node: EXPAND (too broad -> split), REVISE "
393	                "(weakly supported -> refresh), or CONTRACT (redundant -> merge). Reply with one word.\n\n"
394	                f"NODE: {node['title']}\nQUESTION: {question}"
395	            ).strip().upper()
396	            gained = 0
397	            if "EXPAND" in op:
398	                children = _lines(
399	                    self.llm(f"Split this section into 2-4 finer sub-sections (titles, one per line): {node['title']}"),
400	                    cap=4,
401	                )
402	                for ch in children:
403	                    rows = self._search(retrieve, f"{ch} {question}")
404	                    gained += len(rows)
405	                    nodes.append({"title": ch, "ctx": node["title"], "n": 0, "util": 0.0})
406	                    if not self._budget_left:
407	                        break
408	            elif "CONTRACT" in op:
409	                pass  # reorganize only; retrieves nothing
410	            else:  # REVISE (default)
411	                rows = self._search(retrieve, f"{node['title']} {node['ctx']}")
412	                gained += len(rows)
413	            node["n"] += 1
414	            # utility ~ retrieval gain (normalized); update running mean (eq.7)
415	            u = min(1.0, gained / 8.0)
416	            node["util"] += (u - node["util"]) / node["n"]
417	
418	
419	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- PokeeResearch
420	@dataclass
421	class PokeeResearchMethod(_Budgeted):
422	    """PokeeResearch-7B (2510.15862): research<->verification scaffold. Research turns emit a LIST of
423	    queries per call (multi-query); on 'answer', a verification pass re-reads the thread against three
424	    failure modes (incomplete coverage / insufficient evidence / logical error) and re-enters research."""
425	
426	    llm: LlmFn
427	    name: str = "pokeeresearch"
428	
429	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
430	        thread = ""
431	        for _ in range(self._rounds):
432	            if not self._budget_left:
433	                break
434	            # research turn: emit a LIST of queries (multi-query per call) or ANSWER
435	            out = self.llm(
436	                "You are in RESEARCH mode. Emit EITHER a list of search queries (one per line) to "
437	                "gather more evidence, OR the single word ANSWER if you can fully answer now.\n\n"
438	                f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nFINDINGS SO FAR:\n{thread[-2000:] or '(none)'}"
439	            )
440	            if "ANSWER" in out.upper() and len(_lines(out)) <= 1:
441	                # verification: 3 failure modes; on failure, re-enter research with a gap
442	                v = self.llm(
443	                    "VERIFICATION: re-read the findings and judge against (a) incomplete coverage, "
444	                    "(b) insufficient evidence, (c) logical error. If all pass, reply PASS. Else list "
445	                    "search queries to fix the gap (one per line).\n\n"
446	                    f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nFINDINGS:\n{thread[-2500:]}"
447	                )
448	                if "PASS" in v.upper() and len(_lines(v)) <= 1:
449	                    break
450	                qs = _lines(v, cap=5)
451	            else:
452	                qs = _lines(out, cap=5)
453	            for q in qs:
454	                rows = self._search(retrieve, q)
455	                thread += " " + _digest(rows, n=3)
456	                if not self._budget_left:
457	                    break
458	
459	
460	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- DOLORES
461	@dataclass
462	class DoloresMethod(_Budgeted):
463	    """DOLORES (2605.11388): recursive meta-decomposition over {associate, compute, recurse}. The
464	    paper specifies no retriever; faithfully-but-additively, ASSOCIATE nodes emit search strings.
465	    Decompose until atomic or budget; union of retrieved spans is the corpus."""
466	
467	    llm: LlmFn
468	    name: str = "dolores"
469	    max_depth: int = 2
470	
471	    def _run(self, question, retrieve, budget):
472	        self._decompose(question, retrieve, depth=0)
473	
474	    def _decompose(self, task: str, retrieve: RetrieveFn, depth: int):
475	        if not self._budget_left:
476	            return
477	        plan = self.llm(
478	            "Decompose this task into atomic steps. For each step output one line prefixed by either "
479	            "'ASSOCIATE: <a search query to gather evidence>' or 'RECURSE: <a sub-problem to decompose "
480	            "further>'. Keep it small; only the parts that need external evidence.\n\nTASK:\n" + task
481	        )
482	        any_typed = False
483	        for raw in (plan or "").splitlines():
484	            if not self._budget_left:
485	                break
486	            s = raw.strip()
487	            if s.upper().startswith("ASSOCIATE:"):
488	                any_typed = True
489	                self._search(retrieve, s.split(":", 1)[1].strip())
490	            elif s.upper().startswith("RECURSE:") and depth < self.max_depth:
491	                any_typed = True
492	                self._decompose(s.split(":", 1)[1].strip(), retrieve, depth + 1)
493	        if not any_typed:
494	            # the paper leaves the retrieval binding unspecified; faithfully-additive fallback:
495	            # treat each decomposition line as an ASSOCIATE search subgoal.
496	            for q in _lines(plan, cap=6):
497	                if not self._budget_left:
498	                    break
499	                self._search(retrieve, q)
500	
501	
502	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- registry
503	def build_frontier_methods(llm: LlmFn) -> list:
504	    """All 9 runnable frontier adapters, constructed with the shared GLM-5.2 llm."""
505	    return [
506	        WebWeaverMethod(llm=llm),
507	        IterResearchMethod(llm=llm),
508	        ConvergeWriterMethod(llm=llm),
509	        WarpMethod(llm=llm),
510	        DuMateMethod(llm=llm),
511	        FsResearcherMethod(llm=llm),
512	        ScaffoldAgentMethod(llm=llm),
513	        PokeeResearchMethod(llm=llm),
514	        DoloresMethod(llm=llm),
515	    ]
516

content.text: Clean — these are named after real paper/method names (WebWeaver, IterResearch, etc.), legitimate domain terms.

content.content:
1	"""Query-gen method adapters for the coverage-isolation harness (I-qgen-001, GH #1291).
2	
3	Each method's ONLY job: assemble the best corpus via the SHARED retrieve(). Methods decide
4	WHAT to search and WHICH retrieved rows to keep — they never transform evidence text
5	(common POLARIS code owns chunking/dedup/provenance downstream). Isolation: same retrieve()
6	for everyone, only the queries differ.
7	
8	- FloorMethod: current POLARIS query-gen (question anchor + a fixed set of facet queries).
9	- ClosedLoopMethod: the frontier mechanism (WebWeaver/IterResearch family) — decompose the
10	  question into the method's OWN sub-points (never the gold rubrics), seed-retrieve, then
11	  gap-driven re-query. Uses an injected LLM (GLM-5.2 in a real run; stubbable in tests).
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from typing import Callable
18	
19	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_coverage_harness import CoverageBudget, RetrieveFn
20	
21	# (prompt) -> completion text. Wires GLM-5.2 in a real run; stubbable in unit tests.
22	LlmFn = Callable[[str], str]
23	
24	
25	def _dedup_keep(rows: list[dict], seen_urls: set[str]) -> list[dict]:
26	    """Keep rows with a new URL (common-code dedup, identical for every method)."""
27	    out: list[dict] = []
28	    for row in rows or []:
29	        url = (row.get("url") or "").strip()
30	        if url and url not in seen_urls:
31	            seen_urls.add(url)
32	            out.append(row)
33	    return out
34	
35	
36	@dataclass
37	class FloorMethod:
38	    """Current POLARIS query generation: the question anchor + a fixed list of facet queries.
39	
40	    Open-loop / plan-once — exactly the behaviour the diagnosis flagged as two steps behind
41	    the frontier. This is the floor every candidate must beat.
42	    """
43	
44	    facets: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
45	    name: str = "floor_template_facets"
46	
47	    def generate_corpus(
48	        self, question: str, retrieve: RetrieveFn, budget: CoverageBudget
49	    ) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
50	        seen: set[str] = set()
51	        corpus: list[dict[str, str]] = []
52	        for query in ([question] + list(self.facets))[: budget.max_queries]:
53	            corpus += _dedup_keep(retrieve(query), seen)
54	        return corpus
55	
56	
57	@dataclass
58	class ClosedLoopMethod:
59	    """Frontier closed loop: coverage-contract -> seed-retrieve -> gap-driven re-query.
60	
61	    The method builds its OWN coverage contract by decomposing the question (it never sees
62	    the gold rubrics — that would be answer-key leakage). After seeding, it asks the LLM
63	    which sub-topics are still thin and re-queries for exactly those, up to the budget.
64	    """
65	
66	    llm: LlmFn
67	    name: str = "closed_loop_gap_requery"
68	
69	    def generate_corpus(
70	        self, question: str, retrieve: RetrieveFn, budget: CoverageBudget
71	    ) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
72	        seen: set[str] = set()
73	        corpus: list[dict[str, str]] = []
74	        # Budget is enforced by QUERIES ISSUED (not unique URLs) so every method competes
75	        # under the same query budget cap — a query that returns many or few URLs must not
76	        # change how many queries a method is allowed to spend (Codex iter-2 P1).
77	        queries_issued = 0
78	
79	        contract = self._decompose(question)
80	        for query in [question] + contract:
81	            if queries_issued >= budget.max_queries:
82	                break
83	            corpus += _dedup_keep(retrieve(query), seen)
84	            queries_issued += 1
85	
86	        for _ in range(budget.max_query_rounds):
87	            if queries_issued >= budget.max_queries:
88	                break
89	            gaps = self._find_gaps(question, contract, corpus)
90	            if not gaps:
91	                break
92	            for query in gaps:
93	                if queries_issued >= budget.max_queries:
94	                    break
95	                corpus += _dedup_keep(retrieve(query), seen)
96	                queries_issued += 1
97	        return corpus
98	
99	    def _decompose(self, question: str) -> list[str]:
100	        raw = self.llm(
101	            "Decompose this research question into 6-10 specific sub-topics a COMPLETE "
102	            "answer must cover. Return ONE short search query per line, no numbering, no prose.\n\n"
103	            f"QUESTION:\n{question}"
104	        )
105	        return [ln.strip() for ln in (raw or "").splitlines() if ln.strip()][:10]
106	
107	    def _find_gaps(
108	        self, question: str, contract: list[str], corpus: list[dict[str, str]]
109	    ) -> list[str]:
110	        retrieved = "\n".join(
111	            ((row.get("url") or "") + " " + (row.get("text") or "")[:120])
112	            for row in corpus[:60]
113	        )
114	        raw = self.llm(
115	            "Given the question, its sub-topics, and the sources retrieved so far, list up "
116	            "to 4 SHORT search queries for sub-topics still THIN or MISSING. One query per "
117	            "line, no numbering. If coverage is already adequate, return nothing.\n\n"
118	            f"QUESTION:\n{question}\n\nSUB-TOPICS:\n" + "\n".join(contract)
119	            + f"\n\nRETRIEVED SO FAR:\n{retrieved}"
120	        )
121	        return [ln.strip() for ln in (raw or "").splitlines() if ln.strip()][:4]
122
